document.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Health Care Reform Developer Guide

root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Health Care Reform Developer Guide
root.summary: Front matter and legal notices for the Cúram 8.1.2 Health Care Reform Developer Guide, followed by the full contents and the main guide. The section is a navigation/implementation overview for customizing HCR features: adding products and Streamlined Medicaid categories; configuring Eligibility Viewer, Universal Access, appeals, resilient intake, and primary client display; customizing the citizen portal; integrating with external systems/Federal Hub/state systems and electronic verification; case management, plan management, change of circumstances, evidence wizards, appeal requests, closed-case reassessment, trigger points, periodic data matching/annual renewals, inconsistency period processing, FFE account transfer, monitoring, bulk reassessment, and intake address mapping. The end pages include Merative usage terms, export restrictions, warranty disclaimers, patent/trademark notices, and copyright © 2012, 2024.

children.title: Note
children.summary: Introductory note directing readers to the Notices section before using the information or product. Includes copyright notice for Merative US L.P. (2012, 2024).

children.title: Edition
Edition — pages 5-14
children.summary: Front matter and table of contents for the Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2 Health Care Reform guide, followed by the opening of Chapter 1. It introduces adding a product to HCR, with key flow, rule implementation, Eligibility Viewer, Universal Access, annual renewals, and Streamlined Medicaid category setup. The contents also preview major later topics: configuring HCR, portal customization, external systems, case management, plan management, change of circumstances, evidence wizards, appeals, closed cases, trigger points, periodic data matching/annual renewals, account transfer with FFE, monitoring, bulk reassessment, and intake process customization.

children.title: Edition — pages 15-24
children.summary: This section explains how to add a new product to the Cúram HCR solution. It covers creating a program group rule set and product rule class, defining key attributes like product ID, product type, eligibility timelines, coverage dates, and benefit-unit timelines, and delegating eligibility to the Eligibility and Entitlement rule set. It also describes cascade logic changes to prevent overlapping benefits and how to update affected products’ eligibility rules. Finally, it introduces product manager interfaces for managing products and case groups.

children.title: Edition — pages 25-34
children.summary: This section explains how to add a new Health Care Reform product in Cúram: binding `HealthCareProductManager` and `HCRCaseGroupsRuleReference` implementations by product type, configuring Eligibility Viewer/Universal Access, adding eligibility categories and rules, mapping results and messages, and linking UIM fragments. It also covers how projected eligibility updates datastore flags in Universal Access, how to implement annual renewals for QHPs via `manageProgram`, and gives Streamlined Medicaid example code tables, eligibility, and display-rule setup.

children.title: Edition — pages 35-44
children.summary: Pages 35–44 cover two main areas: adding a new Streamlined Medicaid coverage category (“65 or Older”) and configuring Health Care Reform. The category example shows updates to coverage rules, eligibility calculations, and display rules, including new code table value MCT26013, FPL limits, and summary text. The configuration topics include appeals setup, enabling intake workflow resilience, rules for coverage period start dates, customizing the Eligibility Viewer, and primary client display settings.

children.title: Edition — pages 45-54
children.summary: Covers Health Care Reform configuration and customization from pages 45–54. Topics include application properties for displaying primary client/client/member information across case lists, context panels, and person pages; hook points for customizing case client lists; Smart Navigator setup; portal customization for motivations, eligibility display rules, IRS income and income calculations; moving the Log Out button; enabling the Notices tab; and integrating external systems via custom implementations, Guice bindings, and request/response class extensions.

children.title: Edition — pages 55-64
children.summary: Covers integration with external systems in HCR: routing Federal Hub calls, available processors, and setup steps for Federal Hub or custom state-system implementations. Explains electronic verification customization, default verification processors, how to add or override processors via Guice/DMX, and lists supported Federal Hub verification service versions. Also introduces case management customization (dynamic evidence, eligibility rules, conditional verifications) and plan management integration, including the vendor-agnostic adapter interface, key methods, and configuration approach using WSDL-based web services.

children.title: Edition — pages 65-74
children.summary: Covers plan management customization in Cúram: how to implement and bind a custom PlanManagementAdapter, generate web service stubs, and use provided Health Care Reform APIs. Documents configuration properties for vendor URLs and availability, callback URLs for enrollment completion/save/cancel, and an employer enrollment notification batch process. Includes API reference and schema details for retrieveDemographicsAndEligibilityDetails, getEntitlementDetails, getHouseholdSummaryDetails, policyIDAvailable, and updateEmployerEnrollment, with request/response structures and data elements like enrollment, person, address, assistor, employer, and household summary fields.

children.title: Edition — pages 75-84
children.summary: This section explains how to customize Cúram change-of-circumstances processing and related evidence-management wizards. It covers the default CoC process flow, IEG script/data store customization, evidence prepopulators and mappers, rule-set updates, workflow changes, and the auto-activate/manual-review property. It also details how to add, modify, or remove evidence via the summary page using evidenceCoCStatus and dataSubmitted flags. The last pages begin Add a Member wizard customization: custom evidence mappers, Guice bindings, and evidence creation order.

children.title: Edition — pages 85-94
children.summary: Covers customization hooks for Cúram healthcare workflows: evidence management wizards (custom bindings for evidence selection, default start dates, and Re-add a Member behavior), appeal request setup (custom IEG script/schema, datastore prepopulation, PDF/XSL template, and related admin properties), closed-case handling (permanent closure reasons, reassessment strategy, and reassessment hook), and trigger-point customization for outbound account transfers and denial reasons. Useful for implementing or extending guided changes, appeals, case closure processing, or eligibility-trigger logic.

children.title: Edition — pages 95-104
children.summary: Covers implementation of periodic data matching and annual renewals. Key topics: prerequisite storage of existing program group determinations via BulkRunProgramGroupEligibility for upgrades; developer workflow for polling external systems, adding external evidence, projected eligibility, notices, and auto-completion; creating run configurations and selecting cases; using PDMEvidenceMaintenance and Run Case Control Manager APIs; advising on income mismatches; implementing citizen notices with XSL templates, Guice bindings, and notification generators; and calculating APTC for annual renewals using custom evidence, evidence handlers, and PDMBatchEvents.

children.title: Edition — pages 105-114
children.summary: This section explains annual renewals and periodic data matching batch flows in Cúram. It covers custom case selection/polling, projected eligibility, notice generation, and auto-completion batches, including required tables, statuses, parameters, and scheduling around a 30-day response window. It also describes configuration of XML Server load balancing for notices, expiry-day properties for PDM and annual renewals, and step-by-step batch execution. The first pages include implementation guidance for annual renewal projected eligibility, benchmark plan retrieval, evidence updates, and event binding.

children.title: Edition — pages 115-124
children.summary: Covers periodic data matching and annual renewal batch processing for QHP, Medicaid, and CHIP. Explains how to configure and run projected eligibility and auto-completion/annual renewal jobs, required runID parameters, and the need to avoid overlapping runs. Describes Medicaid/CHIP annual renewal flow with custom case selection/polling jobs plus provided Process AR batches, which auto-renew unchanged cases and mark them Automatically Completed. Also includes error triage: failure causes, PDMRunCaseControlFailure reporting, APIs for listing/resetting failed cases, and identifying non-renewed cases for follow-up.

children.title: Edition — pages 125-134
children.summary: Covers batch failure reason codes for periodic data matching/annual renewals (e.g. projected eligibility, evidence copy/activation, outstanding verifications, notice send, deceased primary client/tax filer, unknown cause) and how failures are recovered/reset. Also explains extracting program group determination rule object snapshots to SessionDoc HTML. The main customization topics are storage suppression for identical determinations and building custom projected eligibility evidence handlers: mapping external evidence to rule classes, implementing defineInMemoryRuleClasses() and createRuleObjects(), logging, and rule-object creation strategies.

children.title: Edition — pages 135-144
children.summary: Covers implementing periodic data matching and annual renewals customizations. Key topics: rule-object creation from active and external evidence, filtering evidence by type, cloning/modifying active evidence, handling specified derivation attributes, and replacing or disabling projected eligibility evidence handlers via Guice/module bindings. Also explains enabling projected eligibility trace logging. For annual renewals, it describes customizing completion processing, citizen account contestable evidence types, and modifying home-page/My Updates messages and UI/structs for both PDM and annual renewals.

children.title: Edition — pages 145-154
children.summary: Covers customizing external evidence converters and inconsistency period processing. For evidence converters, it explains how to implement new converters with CommonExternalEvidenceConverter, override creation/modification methods, customize or replace default converters via Guice bindings, and disable converters by overriding convert() to return no evidence. It also introduces inconsistency period processing: custom event handlers for milestone creation, default workflow behavior, key APIs, and the related Evidence Activation batch process and streaming batch.

children.title: Edition — pages 155-164
children.summary: Covers configuring and extending Account Transfer with the Federally Facilitated Exchange in Cúram. Includes activation, batch/online processing, stopping inbound or outbound submission, source dataset selection, sender/state and agency properties, FFE codes, schema links, and date/time formats. Also explains customization hooks for data/entity mappings and outbound responses via persistence events and element mappers. Describes the Web Service Java API for inbound payload processing, COC handling, duplicate identification/routing, mapping behavior, and batch processing of pending COC payloads.

children.title: Edition — pages 165-174
children.summary: Covers Account Transfer configuration and monitoring. Includes batch processing behavior and outbound/inbound event hooks for custom mapping, status codes for FederalExchangeApplication in both FFM→State and State→FFM flows, adding custom entities via EntityMapper and updating the Federal Exchange data store schema, and default Account Transfer workflows. Also introduces Health Care Reform monitoring, with the application intake process, resilient processing, straight-through vs manual authorization, error handling, and evidence sharing to integrated cases.

children.title: Edition — pages 175-184
children.summary: Covers HCR process monitoring and bulk reassessment. Describes evidence brokering failure fallback, program group logic, and deprecated HCR Intake Reports (application/status counts, intervention categories, eligible programs). Explains HCR Intake Process Errors and general Cúram process monitoring views for workflow/deferred errors, suspension, retry, abort, and event-wait override. Also details running BulkICReassessment for open integrated cases after rule/config/rate changes, with report fields, customization options, batch properties, and the linked BulkICReassessmentStream process for multi-stream execution.

children.title: Edition — pages 185-186
children.summary: This section explains how to customize the Health Care Reform intake process, specifically address mapping. It describes extending `curam.healthcare.intake.impl.AddressDataIntakeApplicationListener`, which handles datastore-to-evidence mapping during HCR IEG submission from the portal or caseworker application. It notes that the listener is used in the pre-mapping phase, updates address data in the datastore, and can be customized by overriding `preMapDataToCuram`. An example custom subclass and Guice binding are provided.

Front matter and the full table of contents for the Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2 Health Care Reform guide, then the opening chapter material through the end of the book. The section spans adding HCR products and Streamlined Medicaid categories; configuring Eligibility Viewer, Universal Access, appeals, portals, external systems, case management, plan management, change-of-circumstances, evidence wizards, closed cases, trigger points, periodic data matching/annual renewals, account transfer with FFE, monitoring, bulk reassessment, and intake customization. It is the guide’s navigation map and implementation overview.

children.title: Notices
Privacy policy
children.summary: This section is a legal/notice page covering sample data disclaimer, copyright license for sample programs, Merative privacy policy link, and trademark notices. It states the examples use fictitious names, sample code is provided “AS IS” without warranty or liability, and directs readers to Merative’s privacy policy at merative.com/privacy. It also lists trademarks for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle, Linux, Microsoft, and UNIX, plus copyright © Merative US L.P. 2012, 2024.

children.title: Trademarks
children.summary: Lists trademark and copyright notices for Merative and third-party brands. Identifies Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle/Java, Linux, Microsoft/Windows, and UNIX as trademarks or registered trademarks, and notes that other names may belong to others. Also includes a privacy policy link, a disclaimer that sample programs are provided “AS IS” without warranty, and permission terms for copying/modifying sample code under the copyright license.

Legal notices and usage terms for the publication. Covers personal and enterprise use permissions, preservation of proprietary notices, and restrictions on redistribution or derivative works without Merative consent. States no additional rights are granted, permissions may be withdrawn, and export laws must be followed. Includes an “as-is” warranty disclaimer, patent notice, third-party product/web link disclaimers, and copyright © Merative US L.P. 2012, 2024.

children.title: Contents
children.summary: Table of contents for the Cúram Health Care Reform guide, listing major chapters and subtopics with page references. It covers adding and configuring products, portal customization, integrations with external systems and plan management, change-of-circumstances and evidence wizards, appeal requests, closed case handling, trigger points, periodic data matching and annual renewals, inconsistency period processing, account transfer with the Federally Facilitated Exchange, monitoring, bulk reassessment, and intake process customization. Useful as a navigation index rather than substantive content.

children.title: 1 Adding a product to the Cúram solution for Health Care Reform
1.1 Key business flow for adding a product
children.summary: This section introduces the key business flow for adding a product to the Cúram Health Care Reform solution. It explains that the new product can be configured to support authorization, activation, reassessment, the Eligibility Viewer, and annual renewals like existing HCR products. It also notes prerequisite background on creating Cúram products, the HCR business overview, and the compliancy guide, and mentions that the referenced CustomProduct state benefit is only an example.

children.title: 1.2 Implementing rules
Creating a program group
children.summary: Explains how to create a program group for Cúram Health Care Reform by defining a `StateBenefitProgramRuleSet` extending `AbstractProgramGroupRuleSet` and overriding product-specific attributes. Covers required product metadata (`productID`, `productTypeCode`), eligibility timeline, coverage start/end dates, product delivery start/end dates, participant eligibility, and `benefitUnitTimeline` derived from eligible units. Also shows creating a `StateBenefitCase` and delegating eligibility logic to the Eligibility and Entitlement rule set.

children.title: Modifying eligibility and entitlement rules
children.summary: Explains how to modify eligibility and entitlement rules for a custom HCR product. Notes that the provided Cúram HCR solution does not define objectives, so objectiveType returns an empty list unless customized. Shows a sample RuleSet for StateBenefitProduct/StateBenefitCase, including objectiveTypes as a fixed list of AbstractObjectiveType and the use of the ProductEligibilityEntitlementRuleSet and HealthCareRuleSet extensions.

children.title: Updating cascade logic
children.summary: Explains when and how to update Cúram HCR cascade logic for a new product. If the new product cannot overlap with existing products, its eligibility must be inserted into the cascade so more beneficial products create periods of ineligibility for less beneficial ones. Describes creating a rule calculator to derive the new product’s eligibility timeline, then updating cascade rules for affected lower-ranked products (for example HCR CHIP, State Basic, Insurance Assistance, and Unassisted Qualified Health Program) to reference it.

children.title: Display rules
children.summary: Display rules have no HCR-specific integration requirements. Standard product guidelines apply, and the section recommends reviewing existing products to keep caseworker experiences consistent. This is a brief guidance note rather than a procedural or technical integration section.

Explains how to implement rules for a new Cúram HCR product: apply generic product rules plus HCR-specific integration for Program Group Logic. Covers creating a program group rule set, required product attributes, eligibility and coverage timelines, benefit-unit and participant timelines, and delegating to a StateBenefitCase in the eligibility/entitlement rule set. Also describes modifying eligibility/entitlement rules, handling objectives, updating cascade logic when products cannot overlap, and notes display rules need no HCR-specific integration.

children.title: 1.3 Product management examples
children.summary: Explains product management examples for Cúram HCR. Each product type must have its own product manager, bound to its code table type, to handle product creation, modification, and case group updates. Shows a `HealthCareProductManager` interface with `manageProgram` and `manageCaseGroup`, a Guice `MapBinder` binding product types to implementations, and helper methods for creating product deliveries, updating existing deliveries, and maintaining case groups (including Streamlined Medicaid-specific CG4 updates).

children.title: 1.4 Configuring the Eligibility Viewer
children.summary: This section explains how to configure the Eligibility Viewer for a new product. It states that setup is the same as for other products and refers readers to the general “Configuring the Eligibility Viewer” topic for details. It also notes that no Eligibility Viewer configuration is required specifically for HCR.

children.title: 1.5 Configuring Universal Access
Displaying information on the results page
children.summary: Explains how to display a new product on the Universal Access results page for eligibility determination. Steps include adding an EligibilityCategory code table entry, updating HealthCareDisplayRuleSet.xml to include a new eligibilityResultList member, implementing a stateBenefitTest EligibilityResult in HealthCareReformEligibilityRuleset, and adding Category result logic to map code EC26163 to an SB result with status based on visibility and linked members/objectives.

children.title: Adjust the flow based on the projected eligibility
children.summary: Explains how projected eligibility affects the Health Care Reform flow in the Cúram Universal Access script. It notes that eligibility is determined in CustomFunctionDetermineEligibility via HealthCareReformEligibilityRuleSet EligibilityDeterminator, and that results should only be evaluated once enough information is available. Table 1 maps rule outputs to household/person datastore flags for Medicaid/CHIP, IA, UQHP, and SBHP eligibility, including when to set household-wide “any member eligible” indicators and per-person eligibility booleans.

Explains how to configure Universal Access for a new Health Care Reform product and how projected eligibility affects the script flow. It shows adding an EligibilityCategory code table entry (EC26163 for State Benefit), including the new eligibilityResultList member in HealthCareDisplayRuleSet.xml, creating a stateBenefitTest EligibilityResult in HealthCareReformEligibilityRuleset, mapping the result to an SB display Result and description, and linking a UIM fragment. It also notes eligibility is evaluated in CustomFunctionDetermineEligibility and maps rule outputs to household/person flags.

children.title: 1.6 Implementing annual renewals
children.summary: Explains how to implement annual renewals for Qualified Health Plan products by placing renewal logic in the manageProgram method of the HealthCareProductManager interface. Notes that the Streamlined Medicaid renewal batch cannot be used because of a product type code restriction. Frames annual renewals as a specific case of periodic data matching and points to shared infrastructure and related documentation for custom configuration and extension.

children.title: 1.7 Adding a category to Streamlined Medicaid example
Code table samples
children.summary: Shows sample XML code tables for two code types used in later Streamlined Medicaid eligibility/display rules: CoverageCategory and MedicaidCoverageType. Each sample defines a codetable package, identifier, code value, enabled status, and English description “Sixty Five or Older.”

children.title: Coverage rules samples
children.summary: Shows sample configuration for a Streamlined Medicaid coverage category. It defines a Boolean timeline attribute in `MedicaidCoverageCategoryCalculator` using `AgeRangeCalculator` to detect members aged 65–200, and adds a corresponding `CoverageCategoryHelper` entry in `InsuranceAffordabilityIntegratedCase` with a Medicaid coverage type code and FPL limit reference. Introduces eligibility rule samples that rely on previously defined code table types.

children.title: Eligibility rules samples
children.summary: Explains how to extend Streamlined Medicaid eligibility logic for a new 65+ coverage category. Shows adding a Boolean timeline attribute to the coverage category calculator, adding the category to coverageCategoryHelperList with an FPL limit, and updating financial rule calculators. Examples cover hasPassedFinancialRulesTimelineWithDisregard and hasPassedFinancialRulesTimelineWODisregard, using MedicaidCoverageType MCT26013, age-range timeline logic, and a specific upper income limit for the new category.

children.title: Display rules samples
children.summary: This section shows display-rule samples for a Streamlined Medicaid category extension. It demonstrates how to add a new coverage category (MCT26013) into summary display logic using when/equals conditions, ResourceMessage keys, and a properties file. Examples include updating category details, category type, and eligibility criteria display for the “65 or Older” category, plus the corresponding text in StreamlineMedicaidDisplay.properties. Useful for implementing or reviewing UI/rules changes tied to Medicaid coverage category display behavior.

Explains how to add a new “65 or Older” category to Streamlined Medicaid. Covers required code tables, coverage rules, eligibility rules, and display rules. Samples show a new CoverageCategory and MedicaidCoverageType code (MCT26013), age-based Boolean timeline logic using AgeRangeCalculator (65–200), adding the category to coverageCategoryHelperList with an FPL limit, updating financial eligibility checks, and wiring UI summary/criteria text via ResourceMessage keys and StreamlineMedicaidDisplay.properties. Useful for implementing or reviewing Medicaid category extensions.

Explains how to add a new product to Cúram HCR so it can be authorized, activated, reassessed, viewed, and renewed like existing products. Covers prerequisites, program group rule setup, required product attributes, delegation to eligibility/entitlement rules, product manager examples, Eligibility Viewer and Universal Access configuration, annual renewals, and a Streamlined Medicaid category extension example. The example shows adding a “65 or Older” category through code tables, coverage/eligibility rules, and display resources.

children.title: 2 Configuring Health Care Reform
2.1 Configuring appeal requests
Appeal case types and appeals process configuration
children.summary: Explains how to configure appealability in HCR. It notes that, if using Cúram Appeals, all application programs must be set to “Appeal All Programs = true” in Universal Access > Application Cases. It also states that HCR case types are appealable by default when Appeals is licensed, including denied applications, product delivery cases, and determinations. The default appeals process has three stages: Stage 1 = Any, Stage 2 = Hearing Review, and Stage 3 = Judicial Review.

Explains how to configure appeal requests in HCR when using Cúram Appeals. Administrators must set “Appeal All Programs” to true for application cases in Universal Access > Application Cases so programs of any status can be appealed. Also notes that, by default, HCR case types are appealable when the Appeals module is licensed, including denied applications, product delivery cases, and determinations. The default appeals process has three stages: Stage 1 = Any, Stage 2 = Hearing Review, and Stage 3 = Judicial Review.

children.title: 2.2 Configuring the resilient option for the process intake application workflow
children.summary: This section explains how to enable the resilient option for the process intake application workflow after installing version 6.0.5.5 or later. It instructs administrators to log into Cúram Administration and set `curam.intake.use.resilience` to `true`, which provides a more granular workflow and improved error handling.

children.title: 2.3 Configuring rules to determine the coverage period start date
children.summary: Explains how to configure the `curam.hcr.effectivedate` environmental property to control how a change of circumstance determines the coverage period start date. `YES` (default) uses the date the evidence was received; `NO` uses the evidence effective date. Applies across all programs and determines whether the reported change is treated by received date or effective date.

children.title: 2.4 Configuring the Eligibility Viewer
Configuring a product for the Eligibility Viewer
children.summary: Explains how to configure a product for the Eligibility Viewer in the Insurance Affordability (CT26301) integrated case. It notes that all products are already displayed by default, and you can add a new product, replace an existing product, or change its display color using PRODUCTTYPE-based configuration. The section describes implementing EligibilityProductDataRetriever, binding it with Guice via a MapBinder keyed by product type, and gives an example for replacing Insurance Assistance. Also warns administrators to maintain performance and scalability of custom bindings.

children.title: Configuring look back and look forward periods
children.summary: Explains how to configure the Eligibility Viewer’s look back and look forward periods by updating `TimelineCalendar.properties` properties `num.years.to.look.forward` and `num.years.to.look.back`. Default Health Care Reform retrievals are limited to one year before and after the displayed year to improve performance. Notes these settings also affect Income Support products such as Traditional Medicaid, Food Assistance, and Cash Assistance.

Configures the Eligibility Viewer for Health Care Reform cases. It explains that caseworkers can view eligibility for people or integrated cases, while administrators can customize displayed products, colors, and key event messages. For products in the Insurance Affordability (CT26301) integrated case, each uses a Java data retriever tied to a PRODUCTTYPE code table value; admins can add or replace implementations via Guice MapBinder bindings, with performance/scalability caution. Also notes that display-rule changes may require product implementation updates.

children.title: 2.5 Configuring primary client display settings for Health Care Reform
Configuring Smart Navigator for Health Care Reform
children.summary: Explains how Smart Navigator is configured for Health Care Reform. It lets caseworkers navigate quickly to predefined persons, pages, or tabs, and Income Support for Medical Assistance is already set up to use it. Organizations can enable or disable Smart Navigator per application role and customize search targets and keywords. The section points to related documentation for using Smart Navigator and customizing it further.

Explains how to configure Health Care Reform primary client display behavior through application properties. Covers hiding or replacing “Primary Client” with “Client(s)” on case list pages, integrated case home pages, context panels, case search participant modals, integrated case tabs/headers, and person pages. Also notes a hook point for customizing which clients appear in case lists via Guice, with default behavior differing by case type. Ends with Smart Navigator setup for Health Care Reform, including role-based enable/disable and custom search targets/keywords.

Covers Health Care Reform configuration tasks: enabling appeal requests for all programs, turning on the resilient intake workflow (`curam.intake.use.resilience=true`), and choosing whether coverage starts from evidence received date or effective date (`curam.hcr.effectivedate`). Also explains Eligibility Viewer setup, including product/color customization, key events, and look-back/look-forward tuning. The rest details primary client display settings across case, context, search, integrated case, and person pages, plus hook-point customization, and Smart Navigator enablement/custom search targets.

children.title: 3 Customizing the Health Care Reform portal
3.1 Customizing the Health Care Reform Motivations
IEG scripts customization
children.summary: This section explains how to customize the Health Care Reform portal’s IEG scripts and related eligibility display rules. It notes that default portal scripts are in the HCROnline component and should be customized by creating a custom copy. It also describes Health Care Reform motivations, their link to IEG scripts, data store schemas, and display rule sets, and lists the default motivations. It highlights that the eligibility results page uses display rules and that the curam.healthcare.eligibility.ruleset.name property must be updated when using custom eligibility rules.

children.title: Eligibility Display Rules customization
Customizing the conditional display of IRS income information
children.summary: Explains how to customize eligibility rules in Cúram 8.1.2 for IRS income display and question flow. For IRS income information, retrieved income is hidden when there are multiple financial households, any American Indians/Alaskan Natives, or household income is below Medicaid/CHIP thresholds; override by creating a custom class extending `AbstractEligibilityRuleset.DefaultIRSIncomeDisplayDeterminator` and updating `curam.healthcare.displayirsincome.invoking.ruleclass.name`. Also covers customizing which Medicaid/CHIP/IA questions are asked by creating a custom `EligibilityDeterminationCalculator` class extending `DefaultEligibilityDetermination`.

children.title: Customizing the conditional display of specific questions for Medicaid, CHIP, or IA
children.summary: Explains how to customize eligibility rules that control which questions are shown for Medicaid, CHIP, or IA, and also notes related IRS income display rules. It says IRS income is hidden when there are multiple financial households, any American Indians/Alaskan Natives, or household income is below Medicaid/CHIP thresholds, via the IRSIncomeDisplayDeterminator in the HealthCareReformEligibilityRuleset. To customize, create a rule class extending the default abstract rule classes and update the relevant property to the custom class name.

children.title: Customizing the determination of projected annual income for a citizen
children.summary: Explains how to customize projected annual income calculation in the Health Care Reform portal. The default rule is driven by `curam.healthcare.memberincome.invoking.ruleclass.name`, which points to `HealthCareReformEligibilityRuleset.MemberIncomeCalculator`. To override it, create a custom rule class following `AbstractEligibilityRuleset.MemberIncomeCalculator` and extending `AbstractEligibilityRuleset.DefaultMemberIncomeCalculator`, then update the property to the custom class’s fully qualified name. Also notes that citizens may attest to or override the calculated income value.

Explains how to customize Health Care Reform portal eligibility display rules. It describes when IRS income retrieved from the IRS is hidden (multiple financial households, American Indians/Alaskan Natives, or household income below Medicaid/CHIP thresholds) and how to override it by extending `AbstractEligibilityRuleset.DefaultIRSIncomeDisplayDeterminator` and updating `curam.healthcare.displayirsincome.invoking.ruleclass.name`. It also covers customizing script question flow for Medicaid, CHIP, or IA by extending `AbstractEligibilityRuleset.DefaultEligibilityDetermination`.

Explains how to customize Health Care Reform portal motivations, which link an IEG script, data store schema, and display rule set. Lists default motivations (Find Assistance, Browse for plans, Quick Shopping, Employer Sponsored Coverage, Apply for an exemption) and notes default scripts are in HCROnline and should be copied for customization. Covers eligibility display rules, including updating `curam.healthcare.eligibility.ruleset.name` for custom rules. Also describes custom IRS income display logic and program-specific question flow (Medicaid/CHIP/IA) via rule-class extension and property updates.

children.title: 3.2 Moving the Log Out button to improve usability
children.summary: Explains how to improve usability by moving the Log Out button from the Welcome Person drop-down to the Universal Access mega-menu in a custom Citizen Portal. Notes that the displayed “LogOut” text comes from the banner-menu title attribute in the associated CITWSAPP.properties file. Also states you can choose whether to keep or remove the old Log Out option from the welcome-person menu.

children.title: 3.3 Enabling the Notices tab
children.summary: Explains how to manually enable the Notices tab in a custom Citizen Portal (Universal Access), since it is shown by default only in Universal Access. The tab lets citizens view all communications sent to or from the agency on the navigation bar. Instructions are to edit the custom CitizenAccount.nav file with the specified navigation-page entry and update CitizenAccount.properties with required properties.

Explains how to customize the Health Care Reform citizen portal. Covers default motivations and how to copy HCROnline IEG scripts, update eligibility display rules and properties, and override rules for IRS income display, Medicaid/CHIP/IA question flow, and projected annual income via custom rule classes. Also describes moving the Log Out button to the Universal Access mega-menu by editing CITWSAPP.app/properties, and manually enabling the Notices tab in a custom Citizen Portal by updating CitizenAccount.nav and properties.

children.title: 4 Integration with external systems
4.1 Customizing the external system implementations
Customizing request or response fields for external system calls
children.summary: Explains how to customize external system call request/response fields in Cúram. To do so, extend the standard request or response detail classes, add custom attributes and accessors, then use those updated classes in a custom implementation of the external system interface. The section also reiterates the broader extension pattern: create a custom class from the default implementation, bind it with Guice, and register the module in a custom MODULECLASSNAME .DMX file.

Explains how to customize external system integrations in Health Care Reform/Cúram. Customers can replace default Federal Hub-related service implementations by extending the standard implementation class, binding the custom class to the interface with a Guice module, and registering that module in a custom MODULECLASSNAME .DMX file. Also shows how to customize request/response payloads by extending the relevant request or response detail classes, adding custom fields and accessors, then using those updated classes in the custom interface implementation.

children.title: 4.2 External system processors
children.summary: Explains external system processors used during application processing to call out to external systems and store returned data for later electronic verification. Gives an example using CombinedSSAServiceViewProcessor to verify SSN and citizenship via the Federal Hub. Lists the default available processors (SSA, income, lawful presence, MEC, RIDP variants). Also notes that external calls are routed to empty implementations by default and describes configuring the Federal Hub by setting three properties and restarting the server.

children.title: 4.3 Configuring the Federal Hub implementation
children.summary: Explains how to route external system calls to Federal Hub implementations instead of default empty stubs. To enable this, set curam.healthcare.test.registerMockExternalSystems to false, set curam.fed.hub.verification.system.name to the Federal Hub system name, and set curam.fed.hub.verification.system.registered to true, then restart the server. Also notes that configuration properties are covered in the Cúram System Configuration Guide.

children.title: 4.4 Configuring a State systems implementation
children.summary: Explains how to configure a custom State systems implementation that can call a state system first and then fall back to the Federal Hub, e.g. for Current Income. Begins the next topic on customizing electronic verifications, noting that external systems support verification data sources and that default processors exist for items like Citizenship, Residency, and SSN, with support for custom or overridden verification logic.

children.title: 4.5 Customizing electronic verifications
Default verification processors
children.summary: Explains default verification processors in Health Care Reform’s electronic verification framework. It notes that verification processing can be customized or overridden, and that seven processors are available by default. The excerpt begins listing processors such as ResidencyVerificationProcessor and CitizenshipVerificationProcessor, describing how they determine verified status by comparing person attributes with data returned from external systems. Useful for understanding built-in electronic verification behavior and where to extend it.

children.title: Adding custom verification processing
children.summary: This section explains how online verification processors determine when applicant data is considered verified for various types such as citizenship, incarceration, SSN, income, MEC, and ESI, using external-system data entities and case applicant records. It then outlines how to add custom verification processing: add a new VerificationItemType code in CT_VerificationItemType.ctx, implement the VerificationProcessor interface with getVerificationType() returning that code, and register the implementation through a custom Guice module using a Set Multibinder.

children.title: Overriding the default verification processing
children.summary: Explains how to override default online verification processing in Cúram. To customize a verification item type, first identify the code in CT_VerificationItemType.ctx, then implement curam.hcr.verification.online.impl.VerificationProcessor so getVerificationType() returns that code. Register the implementation in a custom Guice module using a Set Multibinder, and add the module name to the ModuleClassName .DMX entry. The page also introduces supported federal hub verification services, noting only certain versions are supported.

Describes how Health Care Reform electronic verifications can be customized or overridden. It lists the seven default verification processors (Residency, Citizenship, Incarceration, Household SSN, Income, MEC, ESI) and explains that each verifies applicant data by comparing case/person attributes with external-system data. It then gives the basic extension path: add a VerificationItemType code, implement VerificationProcessor with getVerificationType() returning that code, and register it via a custom Guice module/Set Multibinder.

children.title: 4.6 Supported federal hub verification services
children.summary: Lists the federal hub verification services supported by HCR and the exact sprint versions required. Supported services include Remote ID Proofing (Sprint 14), SSA Composite (Sprint 15), Verify Lawful Presence/VLP (Sprint 15), Verify Employer Sponsored Insurance/ESI (Sprint 14), Verify non-ESI Minimum Essential Coverage (Sprint 15), Verify Current Income (Sprint 14), and Verify Annual Income (Sprint 15). Useful for checking compatibility of verification integrations.

Covers Health Care Reform integrations with external systems: default vs Federal Hub implementations, how to replace implementations via Guice/DMX, and how to extend request/response payload classes. Describes external system processors that call out during application processing and store data for later verification, plus how to route calls to Federal Hub or a custom State-first implementation. Explains electronic verification customization/override, including default processors and extension steps. Lists supported Federal Hub verification services and required Sprint versions.

children.title: 5 Customizing case management
5.1 Dynamic evidence customization
children.summary: This section explains that Health Care Reform (HCR) case management artifacts can be customized, including dynamic evidence, eligibility rule sets, and conditional verifications. For dynamic evidence, it states that HCR provides default dynamic evidence configurations in the HCR component to model data captured for ACA programs, and refers readers to the Cúram Dynamic Evidence Configuration Guide for details. It also begins related customization guidance for eligibility rules and non-financial failure reasons.

children.title: 5.2 Eligibility rules customization
Customizing non-financial rules failure reasons
children.summary: Explains how to customize non-financial eligibility failure reasons in Health Care Reform case management. The failure reason text is stored in the CT_HCIneligibleReason.ctx code table file, and updating the display description does not change the eligibility rule interface contract or require rule changes. Failure reasons are picked up automatically. Includes the file path to edit: EJBServer\components\HCR\codetable\CT_HCIneligibleReason.ctx.

Explains how to customize Health Care Reform eligibility rules and non-financial failure reasons. The section points to default HCR rule sets and references the Cúram eligibility, entitlement, and compliancy guides for rule customization guidance. It also notes that non-financial eligibility failure reason text can be changed in EJBServer\components\HCR\codetable\CT_HCIneligibleReason.ctx, and that updating the display description does not alter the rule interface contract or require rule changes because failures are picked up automatically.

children.title: 5.3 Conditional verifications customization
children.summary: This section says Health Care Reform application cases and integrated cases use the verification framework, and that conditional verification rule sets can be customized like other rule sets. It points readers to the Cúram Verification Guide for verification/conditional verification configuration details and to the Cúram Development Compliancy Guide for compliant customization of default rule sets.

This section explains how to customize Health Care Reform case management artifacts: dynamic evidence, eligibility rule sets, non-financial eligibility failure reasons, and conditional verifications. It notes that HCR includes default dynamic evidence and eligibility rule sets for ACA programs, and points to the relevant Cúram guides for customization and compliant changes. It also explains that non-financial failure reasons can be updated in CT_HCIneligibleReason.ctx without changing the rule interface contract, because failures are picked up automatically.

children.title: 6 Customizing plan management
6.1 Integration with Plan Management
children.summary: Explains how Cúram integrates with an external plan management vendor for insurance affordability assistance. Integration uses both UI and web services: the vendor UI appears in an inline frame, and data exchanges occur through Cúram-owned inbound services and vendor-owned outbound services. Notes that Cúram is vendor-agnostic and does not provide a plan management adapter implementation, so each project must build its own. Also introduces the plan management adapter interface and recommends extending the provided default implementation.

children.title: 6.2 The plan management adapter interface
Configuring the plan management adapter
children.summary: Explains how to configure a custom plan management adapter, typically using web-service stubs generated from a vendor WSDL. The procedure starts by creating an axis directory in a custom component and adding a ws_outbound.xml file that references the vendor’s WSDL. The surrounding interface context lists adapter methods such as retrieving enrollment, employer plan, benchmark plan, policy, and open enrollment details, plus updating entitlement and handling re-enrollment.

Defines the custom plan management adapter interface that projects must implement for vendor-agnostic integration with plan management systems. It outlines the default `PlanManagementAdapterDefault` extension point and the main methods for enrollment, benchmark plans, employer plans, policy IDs, entitlement updates, plan updates, and re-enrollment/continuation. It also notes how enrollment evidence mapping can be customized. Configuration is typically done via web-service stubs generated from the vendor WSDL, using an `axis` directory and `ws_outbound.xml` in a custom component.

children.title: 6.3 Plan management web services provided by Cúram
children.summary: Cúram provides plan management web services that vendors must call to populate screens and support processing. These include retrieving household demographics and eligibility, household summary details, entitlement details, checking whether a policy ID is available, and updating employer enrollment. The section identifies the main interface as curam.planmanagement.adapter.intf.HealthCareWebService and points readers to the HCR component Javadoc for details.

children.title: 6.4 Configuration parameters for plan management
children.summary: Defines plan management integration settings and callback endpoints. Key properties include vendor URLs for the main find-assistance flow, browse-only plan shopping, and employer coverage shopping, each appending a unique enrollment identifier. Also includes a boolean flag indicating whether a plan management vendor is available, defaulting to false for testing and required true in production integration. The section also introduces callback URLs used by the vendor to return control to Cúram after enrollment completion or save-and-exit.

children.title: 6.5 Callback URLs for plan management
children.summary: Defines default callback URLs used by a plan management vendor to return users to the Cúram UI after enrollment actions. Two URLs are listed: one for successful enrollment completion, redirecting to the finishEnrollment page, and one for “save and exit,” redirecting to the saveAndExitEnrollment page so the user can resume later. Useful if you need the exact return endpoints for plan management integration.

children.title: 6.6 Batch processing for plan management
Employer enrollment notification batch process
children.summary: Describes the Employer enrollment notification batch process for plan management. It scans active EmployerEnrollment records, calls the plan management vendor via curam.planmanagement.adapter.impl.PlanManagementAdapter.getEmployerOpenEnrollmentDetails(), and generates/stores pro-forma communications for employees returned by the vendor. The process is used to notify employees that their employer’s open enrollment period is about to begin.

This section describes the plan management batch process available in Cúram, specifically the Employer enrollment notification batch process. It scans active EmployerEnrollment records, calls the plan management vendor via curam.planmanagement.adapter.impl.PlanManagementAdapter.getEmployerOpenEnrollmentDetails(), and generates/stores pro-forma communications for each employee returned. Its purpose is to notify employees that their employer’s open enrollment period is about to begin.

children.title: 6.7 Plan management web service API reference
Health Care Reform web services
retrieveDemographicsAndEligibilityDetails
children.summary: Defines the `retrieveDemographicsAndEligibilityDetails` plan management web service. A vendor uses it to request eligibility information for an enrollment, and Cúram returns the eligibility details plus person-level details for each member in the enrollment. This section sits in the web service API reference for Health Care Reform/plan management integrations.

children.title: getEntitlementDetails
children.summary: Describes the getEntitlementDetails operation for a plan management vendor calling the Health Care Reform income support service to retrieve updated entitlement details for an existing enrollment. The request uses EnrollmentDetails containing the enrollment ID. The response returns EntitlementUpdateDetails with the updated tax credit amount.

children.title: getHouseholdSummaryDetails
children.summary: Describes the getHouseholdSummaryDetails API call in Cúram 8.1.2. A plan management vendor notifies the Income Support for Medical Assistance (Health Care Reform) solution of a status change in an existing enrollment, such as when a carrier finishes processing and provides a policy ID. The request uses EnrollmentDetails and contains the enrollment ID. This section sits alongside related eligibility and entitlement update operations and indicates the service supports enrollment status updates and household summary details.

children.title: policyIDAvailable
children.summary: Describes the policyIDAvailable API for plan management vendors to notify Income Support for Medical Assistance that a carrier has finished processing an enrollment and a policy ID is available. The request contains EnrollmentDetails with the enrollmentID. The surrounding section also briefly references related APIs, updateEmployerEnrollment and its EmployerEnrollmentReceived response, plus the Health Care Reform schema element EnrollmentDetails.

children.title: updateEmployerEnrollment
children.summary: Describes the updateEmployerEnrollment API for notifying the agency that an employer’s open enrollment period has begun. The request contains an EmployerEnrollment object with the employerEnrollmentID, and the response returns EmployerEnrollmentReceived, an indicator of successful receipt and storage of the employer identifier. The section also sits within the Health Care Reform schema overview and follows nearby API tables for enrollment processing.

Health Care Reform web services lists the plan management APIs available to vendors for Cúram Income Support for Medical Assistance. It defines retrieveDemographicsAndEligibilityDetails for requesting enrollment eligibility plus person-level details, getEntitlementDetails for retrieving updated entitlement/tax credit details for an existing enrollment, and getHouseholdSummaryDetails for notifying changes to an enrollment’s status (e.g., carrier processing complete, policy ID available). Each operation uses EnrollmentDetails with the enrollment ID and returns the corresponding update/eligibility response.

children.title: Health Care Reform schema elements
children.summary: Defines the Health Care Reform schema elements used by plan management APIs. Covers core request/response structures such as EnrollmentDetails, EligibilityAndDemographicDetails, HouseholdSummaryDetails, EmployerEnrollment, EmployerEnrollmentReceived, and EntitlementUpdateDetails. Details nested objects for eligibility, persons, addresses, tax filer relationships, previous enrollments, enrollees, assistors, employer details, and phone numbers. Includes key fields and enumerated values for program, enrollment period, gender, coverage category, and relationship type. Useful as a field-level data model reference for enrollment and eligibility integrations.

This section is the API reference for Health Care Reform plan management web services and their data schema. It documents vendor-facing operations: retrieveDemographicsAndEligibilityDetails, getEntitlementDetails, getHouseholdSummaryDetails, policyIDAvailable, and updateEmployerEnrollment. It explains request/response payloads based on EnrollmentDetails, EmployerEnrollment, HouseholdSummaryDetails, and related types. The schema tables define fields for eligibility, persons, addresses, tax-filer relationships, previous enrollments, assistors, employer details, phone numbers, and entitlement updates, including key enumerations for program, enrollment period, gender, coverage category, and relationship type.

Cúram’s plan management customization covers vendor integration for insurance affordability assistance. It uses inline-frame UI plus bidirectional web services, with Cúram remaining vendor-agnostic and requiring project-specific adapter implementation via `PlanManagementAdapterDefault`. The section explains adapter methods, vendor WSDL stub setup, Cúram-provided web services, configuration properties and callback URLs, employer open-enrollment batch processing, and a detailed API/schema reference for health care reform plan management messages and data types.

children.title: 7 Customizing change of circumstances
7.1 Change of Circumstances Process Flow
Change of Circumstances workflow
children.summary: This section describes the default Change of Circumstances workflow for customizing Insurance Affordability updates. It explains how clients review current evidence, use Update My Information when no other change is pending, and enter an IEG script with pre-populated data to add, update, or end-date evidence. It also covers submit processing: HCR online and special enrollment rules generate results, and Life Event infrastructure marks the event pending and starts the workflow. It notes where to view the workflow in the Administration Workspace/Process Definition Tool.

Explains the default Change of Circumstances process flow for HCR insurance affordability updates. Covers how a client views read-only current evidence in the portal, uses Update My Information only when no other change is pending, and completes a pre-populated IEG script to add, update, or end-date evidence. Describes submit handling: HCR online and optional special enrollment rules generate results, then Life Event infrastructure marks the event pending and starts the workflow. Notes where to view the workflow in Administration Workspace/Process Definition Tool.

children.title: 7.2 Customizing the default change of circumstances implementation
Customizing the change of circumstances IEG script
Adding custom entities through the change of circumstances script
children.summary: Explains how to support custom entities in change-of-circumstances scripts. For adding entities, it requires an Add link, a new data store entity, an evidenceCoCStatus attribute using EVIDENCECOCSTATUS (ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED), and calling UpdateEvidenceCoCStatus after submission; the status can drive display conditions and evidence updates, and ADDED may also be inferred from a missing localID. For modifying entities, it requires a Change link, setting evidenceCoCStatus to MODIFIED via change-detection functions, and using a dataSubmitted Boolean plus SetDataSubmitted to manage validation and status reset.

children.title: Modifying entities through the change of circumstances script
children.summary: Describes how to adapt a change-of-circumstances script to add or modify custom entities. For additions, it requires an Add link, a new data store entity with an evidenceCoCStatus attribute using EVIDENCECOCSTATUS (ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED), and setting ADDED via UpdateEvidenceCoCStatus after the IEG page submits. For modifications, it requires a Change link, setting evidenceCoCStatus to MODIFIED using HasChanged/HasAttrValueChanged, and adding a dataSubmitted flag managed by SetDataSubmitted and UpdateEvidenceCoCStatus.

children.title: Removing entities through the change of circumstances script
children.summary: Explains how to remove entities via the Change of Circumstances script: add a Change link, set evidenceCoCStatus to REMOVED via change-detection validation functions, and use a dataSubmitted boolean to manage validation failures and reset status. Also outlines how to customize the Change of Circumstances workflow by versioning the XML workflow, editing/releasing it with the Process Definition Tool, exporting it to the custom directory, and optionally overriding automatic steps via Guice hook points (PreEvidenceProcessing, CaseAndParticipantProcessing, EvidenceUpdater).

Explains how to customize the change of circumstances IEG script for custom entities. It covers adding an Add or Change link from the summary page, creating a data store entity with evidenceCoCStatus (EVIDENCECOCSTATUS: ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED), and using UpdateEvidenceCoCStatus to set status after submission. For modifications, it adds change-detection validations (HasChanged/HasAttrValueChanged) and a dataSubmitted flag managed by SetDataSubmitted. The status drives display conditions and evidence updates, and ADDED can also be inferred from a missing localID.

children.title: Customizing the Change of Circumstances workflow
children.summary: This section explains how to customize the Change of Circumstances workflow and related removal logic. It outlines steps to remove entities via the script, including adding a Change link, setting evidenceCoCStatus to REMOVED through attribute-change checks, and using a dataSubmitted flag to handle validation failures. It also describes how to customize the default workflow by versioning the XML process definition, editing and releasing it with the Process Definition Tool, exporting it to the custom directory, and overriding automatic steps via Guice hook points such as PreEvidenceProcessing, CaseAndParticipantProcessing, and EvidenceUpdater.

Explains how to customize the default Change of Circumstances implementation: update the IEG script and schema, override life event entries, add custom prepopulator/updater code for new or extended evidence, adjust online and program group rules, and test end-to-end results. It details adding, modifying, and removing evidence in the summary-page-driven script using evidenceCoCStatus (ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED), UpdateEvidenceCoCStatus, HasChanged/HasAttrValueChanged, and dataSubmitted. It also covers workflow customization via versioned process definitions and Guice hook points for automatic steps.

children.title: 7.3 Configuring the change of circumstance evidence submission workflow
children.summary: Configures the Change of Circumstance evidence submission workflow via the property curam.healthcare.coc.auto.activate.evidence. When true (default), submitted evidence is automatically activated on the integrated case if no verifications are outstanding; product delivery cases are reassessed and the life event completes, otherwise a caseworker task is created. When false, evidence is routed for manual review, mapped to the integrated case in in-edit state, and must be manually activated before reassessment. Includes steps to change the property in System Administration.

Explains how to customize Change of Circumstances for HCR. Covers the default process flow: portal read-only evidence, Update My Information, prepopulated IEG updates, submit handling, life event pending, and workflow start. Describes customization steps for IEG scripts/schemas, life event entries, new or extended evidence prepopulators and mappers, online/program-group rules, and workflow versions/hook points. Also documents the submission property curam.healthcare.coc.auto.activate.evidence, which controls automatic evidence activation versus manual review.

children.title: 8 Customizing evidence management wizards
8.1 Customizing the Add a Member wizard
Customizing wizard evidence mappings
children.summary: Explains how to customize evidence creation in the Add a Member wizard by implementing and binding the `HCRAddMemberEvidenceMapper` interface. Covers the four mapper methods (`preMapEvidence`, `shouldCreateEvidence`, `mapEvidence`, `postMapEvidence`), shows how to add a new custom evidence type via a Guice `MapBinder`, and notes that existing mapper behavior can be customized by extending the out-of-the-box implementation and using a linked binding.

children.title: Customizing wizard evidence mapping order
children.summary: This section explains how to customize the order in which evidence is created when finishing the “Add a Member” wizard. It states that evidence creation is driven by `HCRAddMemberEvidenceMapper` implementations, with evidence types dynamically collected from configured static evidence types and then sorted by `curam.healthcare.guidedchanges.sl.impl.HCREvidenceSelection.selectAndSortEvidenceTypes`. Useful for understanding where to change evidence-processing order during wizard submission.

children.title: Customizing wizard evidence dates
children.summary: This section explains how to customize dates used by the Add a Member evidence management wizard. It introduces the `curam.healthcare.guidedchanges.sl.impl.HCRAddMemberWizardStartDate` interface, which controls the default evidence start date and the initial relationship start date values. The default implementation is `HCRAddMemberWizardStartDateDefaultImpl`, and customers can override it by extending the out-of-the-box class and using a Guice linked binding to inject the custom implementation.

Explains how to customize the Add a Member wizard in three areas: evidence mapping, evidence creation order, and wizard dates. It defines `HCRAddMemberEvidenceMapper` with `preMapEvidence`, `shouldCreateEvidence`, `mapEvidence`, and `postMapEvidence`, shows adding new evidence types via Guice `MapBinder`, and customizing OOTB mappers with linked bindings. It also covers sorting evidence via `HCREvidenceSelection.selectAndSortEvidenceTypes` and overriding `HCRAddMemberWizardStartDate` for default evidence and relationship start dates.

children.title: 8.2 Customizing the Re-add a Member function
Customizing default evidence attributes and post-function processing
children.summary: Explains how to customize default evidence values and post-processing for the Re-add a Member wizard. Shows extension of the out-of-the-box HCRReAddApplicant implementation, overriding getReAddedHCRApplicant to set evidence attributes and postReAddApplicant to run extra actions after re-adding a member. Includes sample code using super to reuse default behavior and a Guice binding that swaps the default class for the custom implementation. Also references customizing default evidence start dates via a similar extension-and-binding pattern.

Explains how to customize the Re-add a Member wizard in a custom environment. The section covers the two extensibility points in `HCRReAddApplicant`: `getReAddedHCRApplicant`, which sets evidence attribute values during creation, and `postReAddApplicant`, which runs after the member is re-added. It shows extending `DefaultHCRReAddApplicant`, reusing OOTB behavior with `super`, adding custom logic, and using Guice binding to replace the default implementation. Also references a similar extension-and-binding approach for default evidence start dates.

This section explains how to customize evidence management wizards. For Add a Member, it covers evidence mapping via HCRAddMemberEvidenceMapper (preMapEvidence, shouldCreateEvidence, mapEvidence, postMapEvidence), adding new mappers with Guice MapBinder, overriding OOTB mappers with linked bindings, changing evidence creation order with HCREvidenceSelection.selectAndSortEvidenceTypes, and customizing default evidence/relationship start dates via HCRAddMemberWizardStartDate. For Re-add a Member, it covers HCRReAddApplicant hooks for setting evidence values and post-processing, plus replacing the default implementation with Guice binding.

children.title: 9 Customizing appeal requests
9.1 Setting the appeals requests IEG script and data store schema
children.summary: Explains how to configure appeal request customization in Cúram. The section instructs administrators to set two Citizen Portal Online Appeals properties in System Administration > Property Administration so they point to the custom appeals data store schema and IEG script: curam.citizenworkspace.appeals.datastore.schema and curam.citizenworkspace.appeals.script.id. This follows creation of custom schema/script and related prepopulator work, and is relevant if you need to redirect appeals processing to a customized implementation.

children.title: 9.2 Customizing the appeal request summary PDF document
children.summary: This section explains how to customize the appeal request summary PDF generated when a citizen submits an appeal. It notes that the default PDF summarizes the information entered during the appeal request, and that you can modify the PDF’s XSL template to meet specific requirements. It also identifies the default template file location: CURAM_DIR\EJBServer\components\HCROnline\data\initial\blob\XSLTEMPLATEINST001.xsl.

Explains how to customize appeal requests after installation. It covers creating a custom appeal request IEG script and data store schema, implementing the AppealDatastorePrepopulator, ensuring it returns the correct potential appellants, and updating application properties to point to the custom script/schema. It also notes that the generated appeal summary PDF can be customized by editing its XSL template, with the default template at `CURAM_DIR\EJBServer\components\HCROnline\data\initial\blob\XSLTEMPLATEINST001.xsl`.

children.title: 10 Customizing the handling of closed cases
10.1 Configuring the permanent closure of closed cases
children.summary: Explains how to keep closed cases permanently closed by configuring the `curam.miscapp.productDeliveryReactivateClosedReason` property with a comma-separated list of closed-reason code values. Cases closed for those reasons are not reassessed or reopened; other closed cases may be reassessed and reopened. Also notes adjacent configuration for closed-case reassessment strategy in HCR product delivery cases, with options to not reassess closed cases or automatically reassess all cases.

children.title: 10.2 Configuring the reassessment strategy for closed cases
children.summary: Explains how to configure the reassessment strategy for closed HCR product delivery cases. The default is “Do not reassess closed cases,” but administrators can change it to either “Do not reassess closed cases” or “Automatically reassess all cases” in the Cúram Administration application under the HCR PD Case Home Page’s Published Eligibility Determination rule set. Useful for understanding whether closed cases will be reassessed during processing.

children.title: 10.3 Customizing the reassessment implementation for closed cases
children.summary: By default, product delivery cases closed as “created in error” are not reassessed. This section explains how to change that behavior by customizing the Eligibility and Entitlement engine hook `curam.core.sl.infrastructure.assessment.impl.ReassessEligiblityHook`. It points readers to the “Eligibility and Entitlement Engine Hooks” documentation for more details.

Describes how to customize closed-case handling in Cúram. It covers permanently keeping selected closed cases closed by setting `curam.miscapp.productDeliveryReactivateClosedReason` to specific closed-reason codes, so those cases are not reassessed or reopened. It also explains configuring the reassessment strategy for HCR product delivery cases between “Do not reassess closed cases” and “Automatically reassess all cases” in the Administration application. Finally, it notes how to change the default reassessment behavior for cases closed as created in error by implementing the `ReassessEligiblityHook`.

children.title: 11 Customizing Trigger Points
children.summary: Explains how to customize trigger points for Outbound Account Transfer. Describes a custom event listener for `EligibilityProcessor.EligibilityEvent.sendOutboundApplication(...)` to override the default trigger decision using `customOutcome`, while `currentOutcome` remains read-only. Also covers triggering an Outbound Account Transfer when a state-initiated application is denied for non-procedural reasons, and how to extend the default denial reason list via `EligibilityProcessorMap.getDenialReasonList()` with additional `IntakeProgramApplicationDenialReasonEntry` values.

children.title: 12 Implementing periodic data matching and annual renewals
12.1 Storing all existing program group determinations
BulkRunProgramGroupEligibility batch process
children.summary: Describes the one-time BulkRunProgramGroupEligibility batch process, which reruns program group logic using current evidence and stores determinations in the database. Results are written to CreoleProgGrpDetermination and CreoleProgGrpDeterData, and each active determination updates the CREOLEPROGGRPDETERMINATIONID field on PROGRAMGROUPDETERMINATION. Also includes verification guidance for batch logs: compare Count B, C, D, and E values, and investigate the log if Count E is greater than 0.

Explains how to store existing program group determinations before periodic data matching or annual renewals, especially when upgrading from versions before 6.0.5.5 IF2. It requires running the one-time BulkRunProgramGroupEligibility batch process, which reruns program group logic on current evidence and saves active determinations in CreoleProgGrpDetermination and CreoleProgGrpDeterData, updating PROGRAMGROUPDETERMINATION.CREOLEPROGGRPDETERMINATIONID. Includes SQL checks (Counts A–C) and log verification (Counts D–E), with Count E > 0 indicating an error.

children.title: 12.2 Developer overview of periodic data matching and annual renewals
children.summary: Developer overview of the main implementation tasks for periodic data matching and annual renewals: polling external systems for client data, ensuring authorization and evidence type mappings; adding external evidence to Cúram via API using run configurations; determining projected eligibility and generating notices, including APTC calculations for annual renewals; and running automatic completion batch processes after a defined interval (default 30 days) for periodic data matching or annual renewals. Includes use of external evidence converters and relevant batch processes for QHP, Medicaid, or CHIP.

children.title: 12.3 Polling external systems
children.summary: This section explains polling external systems via trusted data source (TDS) services for periodic data matching. It focuses on keeping client information current to support eligibility and entitlement decisions, such as verifying tax returns, birth/death details, and address data. It notes that TDS services are treated as another source of evidence in Cúram, but periodic matching uses bulk requests for very large volumes of citizens. Cúram provides no direct TDS connection functions; bespoke APIs handle creating large evidence records from bulk responses.

children.title: 12.4 Adding evidence from external systems
Creating a batch run configuration for annual renewals or periodic data matching
children.summary: This section explains that annual renewals and periodic data matching require a mandatory batch run configuration with a unique run ID to link the process. It also describes automatic renewal of Medicaid/CHIP cases where possible, with custom implementation needed for cases that cannot be auto-processed. The section introduces periodic data matching via trusted data source (TDS) services, which query external systems in bulk to update client information and create large volumes of evidence records through bespoke APIs.

children.title: Implementing case selection for a batch run
children.summary: Explains how to set up case selection for a periodic data matching or annual renewal batch run. It stresses that a run configuration must exist before adding cases, since the run ID links batch jobs in the same run and is required for batch processing. It also notes that only required cases should be selected based on process needs, and client consent to data polling and eligibility checks must be verified before adding a case.

children.title: Inserting evidence from external systems with the PDMEvidenceMaintenance API
children.summary: Explains how to use the PDMEvidenceMaintenance API to load external evidence into periodic data matching and annual renewal runs. It creates run evidence control records for each imported static or dynamic evidence item, stores the evidence as In Edit, and links it to the relevant run case control record. Also notes that evidence can optionally be activated via EvidenceControllerInterface, but activation is not required for eligibility or notice generation. Includes a prerequisite to retrieve current client information via a custom TDS service.

This section explains how to add external-system evidence for annual renewals and periodic data matching. It covers creating a mandatory batch run configuration with a unique run ID, selecting the required cases for a run, and using the PDMEvidenceMaintenance API to bulk load static or dynamic evidence returned from TDS services. Evidence is stored as In Edit and linked to the run’s case control records; optional activation via EvidenceControllerInterface is available but not required for eligibility or notices.

children.title: 12.5 Advising caseworkers about income evidence mismatches
children.summary: Explains how to advise caseworkers when annual-renewal income evidence mismatches occur. A sample Advisor rule set (ARIncomeAdvisorRuleSet) compares client-attested income with externally polled evidence; if items are not reasonably compatible, advice appears on the Integrated Case Home and Evidence Dashboard until the caseworker confirms review/verification. Notes that similar advice can be built for other evidence types using Advisor rule sets, with advice category set to AREVDMIS.

children.title: 12.6 Implementing citizen notices
Implementing citizen notice generation
children.summary: Explains how to implement citizen notice generation for periodic data matching and annual renewals. Notices are not generated by default; you must define the notice content, create a custom XSL template, and implement template selection and data retrieval. The template provider should extend PDMNotificationTemplateImpl and be bound via Guice to PDMNotificationTemplate, with template IDs stored in TemplateIDCode. A separate HCRNotificationGenerator implementation populates notice data and triggers generation, optionally extending PDMNotificationDataRetrievalImpl for extra data.

children.title: Implementing the calculation of APTC for inclusion in notices
children.summary: Explains how annual renewals calculate APTC for notice inclusion. Describes creating a new evidence type identical to BenchmarkPlan, adding an evidence handler that maps it to an in-memory BenchmarkPlan rule object, and registering it via Guice/module table. Also covers implementing a PDMBatchEvents postProjectedEligibility event that retrieves BenchmarkPlan details, creates ProjectedBenchmarkPlan evidence, reruns ProgramGroupProjectedEligibility, and reads eligible IA program data to compute the APTC amount for the updated eligibility snapshot.

children.title: Configuring XML server load balancing for notices
children.summary: Explains how to configure XML Server load balancing for notice generation. To distribute large notice volumes and provide failover, set the curam.xmlserver.host and curam.xmlserver.port properties. Hosts are entered as a forward-slash-separated list of computer names, and ports as a matching forward-slash-separated list, with a one-to-one server-to-port mapping. The procedure notes these settings can be configured in the Administration application.

Explains how to implement citizen notices for periodic data matching and annual renewals. Covers defining notice content, creating custom XSL templates, and wiring a PDMNotificationTemplateImpl via Guice and TemplateIDCode, plus an HCRNotificationGenerator that retrieves data (optionally extending PDMNotificationDataRetrievalImpl). Also describes annual-renewal APTC calculation for notice inclusion using custom evidence, an evidence handler, and a PDMBatchEvents postProjectedEligibility implementation that recalculates eligibility and updates the snapshot. Ends with XML Server host/port load-balancing setup for notice generation.

children.title: 12.7 Overview of the periodic data match batch process flow
