children.summary: This section explains the overall periodic data match (PDM) batch flow, which has four discrete but connected steps. It notes that end-to-end batch PDM requires custom batch jobs working with the provided PDMProjectedEligibility and PDMProcessAutoCompletions jobs. Steps 1 and 2 must be custom implementations, including custom case selection and polling processes that identify cases for each run, write them to the PDMRunCaseControl table, and poll external systems for information.

children.title: 12.8 Running the periodic data matching batch processes
PDMProjectedEligibility batch process
children.summary: Describes the PDMProjectedEligibility batch process for periodic data matching, including how to run it with a run configuration/runID and its parameters (runID mandatory, instanceID optional, processingDate optional, runInstanceID internal only). It explains that this process runs projected eligibility and notice generation for specified cases, and notes that evidence inserted via PDMEvidenceMaintanence automatically triggers it. Also introduces PDMProcessAutoCompletions, which must run after the expiry period to redetermine eligibility for cases still in “Notice Generated” state.

children.title: PDMProcessAutoCompletions batch process
children.summary: Describes the PDMProcessAutoCompletions batch process, which finalizes periodic data matching after the configured response period. If a citizen has not contested or confirmed changed information, it redetermines eligibility based on the projected eligibility notice. The process must be scheduled for curam.citizenaccount.periodicdatamatch.expiry.days + 1 days; by default this is 31 days after notice generation. It applies to cases remaining in the “Notice Generated” state.

Explains how to run the two periodic data matching batch processes independently: PDMProjectedEligibility first, then PDMProcessAutoCompletions after the response period. Covers required run configuration/runID, command-line execution, and parameters (runID mandatory; instanceID/processingDate optional; runInstanceID internal only). Notes that evidence inserted via PDMEvidenceMaintanence automatically triggers projected eligibility and notice generation. The auto-completions batch reruns eligibility for cases still in “Notice Generated” status, typically 31 days after notice generation by default.

children.title: 12.9 Configuring automatic completion intervals for periodic data matching
children.summary: This section explains how to change the automatic completion/expiry interval for periodic data matching responses. It states the default expiry period is 30 days and gives the configuration step to modify the `curam.citizenaccount.periodicdatamatch.expiry.days` property in Cúram Administration with administrator access. It also identifies the batch class/method `curam.hcr.pdm.sl.intf.PDMProcessAutoCompletions.process` and its parameters, including the mandatory `runID` and optional `instanceID` and `processingDate`.

children.title: 12.10 Configuring and running the annual renewals batch processes
Configuring automatic completion intervals for annual renewals
children.summary: Explains how to change the default automatic completion expiry interval for annual renewals. The default is 30 days, and administrators can modify the `curam.citizenaccount.annualrenewal.expiry.days` property to match legal or business requirements. Also introduces the QHP annual renewal batch process flow, noting it has four steps and requires custom batch jobs alongside the provided `QHPProjectedEligibility` and `QHPProcessAutoCompletions` jobs.

children.title: Overview of the QHP annual renewal batch process flow
children.summary: Explains the QHP annual renewal batch flow and how to configure renewal expiry timing. The default citizen response expiry is 30 days, configurable via the curam.citizenaccount.annualrenewal.expiry.days property. The annual renewal process consists of four batch steps, with steps 1 and 2 implemented as custom jobs that feed the provided QHPProjectedEligibility and QHPProcessAutoCompletions jobs. Custom Case Selection and Polling batches identify cases, write to PDMRunCaseControl, poll external systems, populate renewal control tables, and load evidence data.

children.title: Running the annual renewals for QHP batch processes
QHPProjectedEligibility batch process
children.summary: Describes the QHPProjectedEligibility batch process used during annual renewals for Qualified Health Plans to run projected eligibility and notice generation for specified cases. Explains that evidence entered via PDMEvidenceMaintenance auto-triggers this processing, but cases with no new evidence may also be included. Lists the batch class/method curam.hcr.pdm.sl.intf.QHPProjectedEligibility.process and parameters such as mandatory runID, optional instanceID and processingDate, and internal runInstanceID. Also outlines QHPProcessAutoCompletions, which completes renewals after the configurable expiry period.

children.title: QHPProcessAutoCompletions batch process
children.summary: Describes the QHPProcessAutoCompletions batch process for annual renewals of Qualified Health Plans. It automatically completes renewals after a configurable waiting period if the citizen has not responded, redetermining eligibility based on the projected eligibility notice. The process should be scheduled after curam.citizenaccount.annualrenewal.expiry.days + 1 days; by default this is 31 days, since the expiry setting is 30 days. All cases in the “Notice Generated” state for that period are processed.

Explains the two-step QHP annual renewal batch flow: QHPProjectedEligibility runs projected eligibility and notice generation for cases in a run, updating process control records and auto-completing renewals when eligibility is unchanged; QHPProcessAutoCompletions later redetermines eligibility and completes cases that remain in “Notice Generated” after the expiry period. Includes run prerequisites, scheduling guidance, and batch details such as the QHPProjectedEligibility.process class/method, required runID parameter, and the default 30+1 day completion timing.

children.title: Overview of the Medicaid annual renewal batch process flow
children.summary: Describes the Medicaid annual renewal batch flow as a three-step process made of discrete, interrelated batch jobs. It explains that end-to-end processing requires custom batch jobs working with the provided MedicaidProcessAnnualRenewals batch job, and that steps 1 and 2 are custom jobs. It also notes that custom Case Selection and Polling batches must identify cases for each Medicaid run, write them to PDMRunCaseControl, poll external systems, populate annual renewal control tables, and populate evidence tables.

children.title: Running the annual renewals for Medicaid batch process
MedicaidProcessAnnualRenewals batch process
children.summary: Defines the MedicaidProcessAnnualRenewals batch process, which runs projected eligibility, sends citizen notices via custom generate notices logic, and auto-renews Medicaid when eligibility is unchanged. Documents the class/method curam.hcr.pdm.sl.intf.MedicaidProcessAnnualRenewals.process and its parameters: mandatory runID, optional instanceID, optional processingDate, and internal runInstanceID. Also introduces the CHIP annual renewal batch flow, noting it has three steps and requires custom batch jobs alongside the provided CHIPProcessAnnualRenewals batch job.

Explains how to run the Medicaid annual renewals batch. The batch performs projected eligibility for each case, sends citizen notices via custom generate-notices logic, and automatically renews Medicaid for cases with unchanged eligibility, marking successful cases “Automatically Completed (RCCS26003)” and failed ones as failed for custom follow-up. Prerequisites include updated external client evidence via PDMEvidenceMaintenance and no overlapping matching/renewal runs. To launch, create or identify a run configuration and run curam.hcr.pdm.sl.intf.MedicaidProcessAnnualRenewals.process with mandatory runID.

children.title: Overview of the CHIP annual renewal batch process flow
children.summary: Describes the CHIP annual renewal batch flow as a three-step process centered on the provided CHIPProcessAnnualRenewals job. It explains that the end-to-end renewal requires custom batch jobs for steps 1 and 2, including custom case selection and custom polling. These custom jobs identify cases for each CHIP run, write them to the PDMRunCaseControl table, poll external systems, populate annual renewal process control tables, and populate evidence tables with retrieved case information.

children.title: Running the CHIP annual renewals batch process
CHIPProcessAnnualRenewals batch process
children.summary: Describes the CHIPProcessAnnualRenewals batch process, which runs projected eligibility, sends citizen notices via the custom generate notices implementation, and auto-renews CHIP when eligibility is unchanged. Lists the main class/method `curam.hcr.pdm.sl.intf.CHIPProcessAnnualRenewals.process` and its parameters (`runID`, optional `instanceID`, optional `processingDate`, internal `runInstanceID`). Also introduces troubleshooting for periodic data matching and annual renewal batch errors, covering full batch failures, per-record failures logged to `PDMRunCaseControlFailure`, and DB-to-JMS processing errors.

Explains how to run the CHIP annual renewals batch. The process performs projected eligibility for each case, sends citizen notices via a custom generate-notices implementation, and automatically renews CHIP for cases whose eligibility is unchanged, marking them Automatically Completed; other cases are set to failed for further custom handling. It advises ensuring current external client data is written as evidence, and warns not to overlap with other periodic data matching or renewal runs. The procedure is to create/identify a run configuration and launch `CHIPProcessAnnualRenewals.process` with `runID`.

Covers configuring annual renewal expiry, with the default 30 days adjustable via `curam.citizenaccount.annualrenewal.expiry.days`. Summarizes batch flows and run steps for QHP, Medicaid, and CHIP annual renewals: custom case selection/polling jobs populate control/evidence tables, then provided batch jobs run projected eligibility, send notices, and auto-complete unchanged cases. Includes prerequisites (current external evidence, no overlapping runs), scheduling guidance, required `runID` parameter, and key batch classes: `QHPProjectedEligibility`, `QHPProcessAutoCompletions`, `MedicaidProcessAnnualRenewals`, and `CHIPProcessAnnualRenewals`.

children.title: 12.11 Triaging periodic data matching and annual renewal batch process errors
Checking for batch processing errors and reprocessing failed cases
children.summary: Explains how to check periodic data matching or annual renewal batch errors and reprocess failed cases. It says error handling is a custom development task, typically implemented with a custom batch or batch-streaming process using the PDMRunCaseControlManager API. The section covers four scenarios: reporting failed cases, reviewing technical failure reasons, resetting technically failed cases for reprocessing, and listing business-failed cases for caseworker follow-up. It also shows APIs to list all failures, technical failures, or business failures by run ID.

children.title: Identifying Medicaid or CHIP cases that were not automatically renewed
children.summary: Explains how to identify Medicaid/CHIP cases that were not automatically renewed after annual renewal runs and reprocess them. Cases may fail because the person is ineligible for the next 12 months or because technical issues prevented projected eligibility determination. Describes using the PDMRunCaseControlManager API and checking the PDMRunCaseControlFailure table or PDMRunCaseControl status to diagnose failures. Includes sample code for reviewing failure details, resetting technical failures, and notifying caseworkers for business failures, plus a query to list failed cases by runID and status.

children.title: Diagnosing PDM and AR batch run failures
PDM and AR batch job failure reasons
children.summary: Lists how to inspect PDMRunCaseControlFailure records for batch failures and explains the failure reason codes in PDM/AR processing. Covers codes FRC001–FRC007 and FRC020, with meanings such as projected eligibility, comparison, evidence copy/activation, outstanding verifications, notice send, deceased primary client/tax filer, and unexpected exception. Describes recovery mainly by correcting invalid evidence/data, then resetting failed case control records via the PDMRunCaseControlManager API so the batch can rerun.

Explains how to diagnose PDM/AR batch run failures using PDMRunCaseControl and PDMRunCaseControlFailure tables/API. Shows queries to list failed cases and inspect failure details, then reset technical failures for rerun or flag business failures for caseworker follow-up. Lists failure reason codes FRC001–FRC007 and FRC020 (projected eligibility/comparison, evidence copy/activation, outstanding verifications, notice send, deceased primary client or tax filer, unexpected exception). Notes recovery by correcting bad data/evidence and resetting case control records via PDMRunCaseControlManager.

Explains how to triage PDM/Annual Renewal batch errors and identify Medicaid/CHIP cases not automatically renewed. Covers three failure modes: complete batch failure, individual case failure, and DB-to-JMS errors. Shows how to use PDMRunCaseControlManager/custom batch-streaming to list failed, technical, or business-failed cases, inspect failure details, reset technical failures, and notify caseworkers for business failures. Includes SQL to query PDMRunCaseControl/PDMRunCaseControlFailure and lists key reason codes FRC001–FRC007 and FRC020, with recovery guidance for some errors.

children.title: 12.12 Extracting rule objects snapshots to SessionDoc style HTML
children.summary: This section explains how to extract a rule objects snapshot as SessionDoc-style HTML by running a build target from the runtime directory. It covers program group determinations, including active or superseded determinations via the CREOLEPROGGRPDETERMINATIONID field in PROGRAMGROUPDETERMINATION, and projected eligibility determinations via the same field in PROGGRPPROJECTEDELIGIBILITY. Useful if you need the extraction method and the database fields/tables involved.

children.title: 12.13 Customizing periodic data matching and annual renewals
Customizing the storage of program group determinations
children.summary: Explains how to customize storage of program group determinations in periodic data matching/annual renewals. From version 6.0.5.5 IF2 onward, determinations are stored in the database by default, but a hook point can suppress storing identical new determinations to reduce table growth. If suppressed, no records are inserted into PROGRAMGROUPDETERMINATION, CREOLEPROGGRPDETERMINATION, or CREOLEPROGGRPDETERDATA. Warns that implementations must still store determinations when needed, since missing updates can affect later comparisons.

children.title: Customizing projected eligibility for periodic data matching and annual renewals
Customizing projected eligibility evidence handlers
Projected eligibility
children.summary: Explains projected eligibility in HCR: rules run using case data plus external data to show how that data would affect eligibility now or in a future period. Describes projected eligibility evidence handlers, which map external evidence into rule objects and can be customized, replaced, disabled, or extended via the ProjectedEligibilityEvidenceHandler API. Warns that custom handlers are non-trivial and must be thoroughly tested. Also notes external evidence should not be directly referenced by eligibility/entitlement rules and may require verification against client-attested values.

children.title: Projected eligibility evidence handlers
children.summary: Explains how to customize projected eligibility evidence handlers to add, replace, or disable mappings from external data to internal rule objects for eligibility projections. Notes that projected eligibility runs HCR Program Group Rules using case data plus trusted external evidence to inform current or future eligibility, and warns that external evidence should not be referenced directly by rule sets. Emphasizes that custom handlers use the ProjectedEligibilityEvidenceHandler API and require thorough testing because errors can cause runtime failures or incorrect eligibility determinations.

children.title: External evidence
children.summary: Defines external evidence as trusted third-party data (for example SSA or IRS) added to an integrated case for eligibility projections only. Explains projected eligibility and projected eligibility evidence handlers, which convert external data into internal rule objects for HCR rule evaluation. Notes handlers can be replaced, disabled, or customized via the ProjectedEligibilityEvidenceHandler API. Warns that direct rule references to external evidence can trigger redeterminations and performance issues, and that custom handlers require careful testing.

children.title: Implementing a new evidence handler
Identifying rule classes for the evidence handler
children.summary: Explains how to identify the rule class an evidence handler must return in `defineInMemoryRuleObjects()`. Warns not to reference external evidence directly in eligibility/entitlement rules because it triggers redeterminations, hurts performance, and blocks projections. Describes the chain to follow: External Evidence → Evidence → Evidence Propagator → Data Rule Set → Data Rule Class → Rule Objects. Gives an example mapping Annual Tax Return/Income Details to Income, then locating the active `ROPT2005` propagator, the associated ruleset, and the final data rule class.

children.title: External evidence to qualified rule class name mappings
children.summary: Explains how external evidence types map to qualified rule class names for custom projected eligibility evidence handlers. It defines the fully qualified rule class name as `RuleSetName.RuleClassName` and says this must be returned by `defineInMemoryRuleObjects()`. Includes default mappings such as Death Status → `HCRApplicantDataRuleSet.HCRApplicant`, Birth and Death Details → `PDCBirthAndDeathDataRuleSet.PDCBirthAndDeath`, Minimum Essential Coverage Benefit → `HCRBenefitDataRuleSet.HCRBenefit`, and Income Details/Annual Tax Return → `IncomeDataRuleSet.Income`. Also introduces projected eligibility rule object creation and notes custom handler naming conventions.

children.title: Rule objects in projected eligibility
children.summary: Explains projected eligibility rule object creation. In projected eligibility, the normal rule object converter is bypassed and the projected eligibility evidence handler creates all in-memory rule objects for rule classes listed by defineInMemoryRuleClasses(). The handler’s createRuleObject() method must replicate the converter’s work, and the developer must choose a rule object creation strategy. Also covers determining the fully qualified rule class name by combining rule set and rule class names, with examples and default mappings from external evidence to rule class names.

children.title: Creating a custom evidence handler
children.summary: Explains how to complete a custom projected eligibility evidence handler by determining the mapped data rule set, rule class name, and fully qualified rule class name to return from `defineInMemoryRuleObjects()`. It notes that rule objects for projected eligibility are created by the evidence handler via `createRuleObject()`, since the normal converter is bypassed. Includes default external evidence-to-rule-class mappings and the naming convention `{Custom Identifier}{Evidence Name}ProjectedEligibilityEvidenceHandlerImpl`.

children.title: Adding logging to custom evidence handlers
children.summary: This section explains how to add logging to custom projected eligibility evidence handlers. It shows using ProgramGroupProjectedEligibilityHelper.log(Level.INFO, message) to record messages and help investigate unexpected eligibility results. It appears within guidance for implementing custom evidence handlers, alongside steps to define in-memory rule classes and create rule objects. Useful if you need debugging support or want to understand how custom handler logging is integrated.

children.title: Implementing the creation of rule objects for projected eligibility
Rule object creation strategies
children.summary: This section explains how `createRuleObject()` should choose data when building rule objects from active and external evidence. It highlights three default strategies: load active internal evidence plus separate external evidence, clone internal rule objects and replace selected attributes with external values, or overlay/effective-date active and external evidence when they are equivalent. It stresses that the right strategy depends on evidence overlap, attribute mapping, and business-defined matching, and may require business analyst input.

children.title: Techniques for filtering evidence and creating rule objects
Filtering external evidence by evidence type
children.summary: Explains how evidence handlers should filter the `EvidenceDescriptorDtlsList` by evidence type before creating rule objects. Shows a custom-handler example using `CollectionUtils.filter` to keep only matching evidence (e.g., `CASEEVIDENCE.MEC`). Also notes that active internal evidence can be converted into rule objects using the default rule object converter, with the relevant Data Rule Set/Class and case ID in the search criteria.

children.title: Creating rule objects from active evidence
children.summary: Explains how default rule object creation strategies merge active internal evidence with external evidence into rule objects, using the Income evidence handler as an example. Describes an income-merging algorithm for when active and external evidence match or differ. Also covers the common technique of filtering incoming evidence descriptor records by evidence type before conversion. Begins describing how active internal evidence can be converted to rule objects via the default converter using the relevant Data Rule Set/Class and case ID.

children.title: Creating rule objects from external evidence
children.summary: Explains how to create rule objects from external evidence for periodic data matching/annual renewals. Shows two patterns: cloning existing internal-evidence rule objects into the current session, or looping through filtered external evidence descriptors, reading dynamic evidence, and populating rule objects in memory. Notes that all rule attributes with specified derivation must be explicitly set, using HCRBenefit/startDate as an example. Includes sample code using rule set/class lookup, evidence conversion, and attribute assignment.

children.title: Cloning and modifying active evidence rule objects
children.summary: Explains how to build a custom evidence handler that clones active evidence rule objects and selectively populates each cloned attribute from either the active evidence record or an external evidence record. The section notes this is useful when records are partially equivalent or when external evidence lacks fields needed by the rule object. It outlines the basic process: load active evidence as rule objects, clone each rule object, then apply business logic per attribute to choose the correct source value.

Explains common techniques used by default and custom evidence handlers to filter evidence and create rule objects. Shows filtering `EvidenceDescriptorDtlsList` by evidence type before conversion, converting active internal evidence via the default rule object converter using the relevant Data Rule Set/Class and case ID, and building rule objects from external evidence by reading dynamic evidence and setting all specified attributes. Also describes cloning active evidence rule objects and selectively merging active and external values, as in the default Income handler’s merge algorithm.

Explains how to implement `createRuleObjects()` for projected eligibility handlers, the most complex step after defining in-memory rule classes. It stresses choosing the right business-driven data strategy: use only active evidence plus external evidence, clone active rule objects and replace selected attributes, or overlay/effective-date matched active and external evidence. It also shows core techniques: filter `EvidenceDescriptorDtlsList` by evidence type, convert active evidence via the default rule object converter using case ID, and build external rule objects by reading dynamic evidence and setting all required specified attributes.

Guidance for implementing a custom projected eligibility evidence handler. It warns external evidence must not be referenced directly in eligibility/entitlement rules because it triggers redeterminations, hurts performance, and blocks projections. It explains how to trace External Evidence → Evidence → ROPT2005 propagator → data rule set → data rule class to identify the fully qualified rule class name returned by `defineInMemoryRuleObjects()`. It also covers default evidence-to-rule mappings, custom handler naming, logging via `ProgramGroupProjectedEligibilityHelper`, and strategies for `createRuleObjects()` using active, external, or merged evidence.

Explains how to customize projected eligibility evidence handlers to add, replace, or disable mappings from external evidence to in-memory rule objects used by HCR projected eligibility. Warns custom handlers are non-trivial and can cause runtime errors or incorrect eligibility results if poorly implemented. Covers the required analysis chain from external evidence to rule class, default evidence-to-rule mappings, handler naming, logging, and `ProjectedEligibilityEvidenceHandler` API use. Also describes three rule-object creation strategies: using only active evidence, cloning/modifying active evidence, or overlaying active and external evidence.

children.title: Customizing an external evidence handler
children.summary: This section explains how to customize or replace the default projected eligibility external evidence handler. It shows how a custom handler maps external evidence fields to rule object attributes by reading values from the dynamic evidence record and specifying them on the rule object. It also describes a cloning approach for active evidence rule objects when records are partially equivalent, using business logic to choose per attribute whether to copy from the existing active evidence or from external evidence.

children.title: Disabling an evidence handler
children.summary: Explains how to disable a projected eligibility evidence handler by replacing the default handler with a custom subclass that contains no implementation. It also covers the Guice binding changes needed to route projected eligibility to the custom handler, including creating or updating a module with a link binding and, if a new module is added, updating the ModuleClassname.dmx file. Useful for customizing or effectively turning off specific evidence handling logic.

children.title: Enabling projected eligibility logging
children.summary: Explains how to enable logging for custom external projected eligibility evidence handlers. It shows replacing the default handler with a custom implementation via a Guice link binding in a module, optionally adding the module to ModuleClassname.dmx. The section notes that external evidence handlers can emit log/trace output during execution to help investigate unexpected projected eligibility results, often alongside tools like SessionDoc, and that this logging is disabled by default.

Explains how to customize projected eligibility for periodic data matching and annual renewals by adding new external evidence types or altering defaults. Covers the full handler workflow: trace external evidence through evidence propagation to the target data rule class, implement `ProjectedEligibilityEvidenceHandler`, and create rule objects from active evidence, external evidence, or a mix of both. Also shows how to replace, disable, or log custom external evidence handlers via Guice bindings and module registration. Warns custom handlers are complex and can cause runtime errors or incorrect eligibility results.

children.title: Customizing the citizen account with new evidence types
children.summary: Guidance on customizing the citizen account after adding new external evidence types. It notes you may need to update citizen choices and decide which evidence types customers can contest. The section also covers customizing annual renewal completion by creating a module class, adding a Guice link binding to replace the default `CompleteAnnualRenewal` implementation, and updating `ModuleClassName.dmx` if a new module is added.

Covers customization of periodic data matching and annual renewals: suppressing storage of duplicate program group determinations via `ProgramGroupDeterminationStorageHook`, and extending projected eligibility to support extra external evidence types. Explains the evidence-to-rule-object chain, how to create rule objects from active or external evidence, and how to replace, disable, or add projected eligibility handlers with Guice/module bindings. Also notes citizen account updates and logging support, with warnings about testing and incorrect eligibility results.

children.title: 12.14 Customizing the completion process for annual renewals
children.summary: Explains how to customize or replace the default annual renewal completion process. You must create a `CompleteAnnualRenewal` implementation extending `AbstractCompleteAnnualRenewal`, then update Guice bindings so the custom class is used instead of the default. The section shows a sample module class with a link binding from `AbstractCompleteAnnualRenewal` to a custom implementation, and notes that if you create a new module class, you must update `ModuleClassName.dmx` to reference it.

children.title: 12.15 Customizing the citizen account for periodic data matching and annual renewals
Configuring contestable evidence types
Adding contestable evidence types to the citizen account
children.summary: Explains how to customize the citizen account for periodic data matching and annual renewals by changing which evidence types are contestable and by editing related messages. By default, Death Status and MEC are contestable for periodic data matching, and income is contestable for annual renewals. To add new contestable evidence types, update the `curam.healthcare.pdm.contestable.evidences` property, add clusters in `CitizenAccount_arpdm.vim`, override `HealthCarePDMProcessingImpl.java`, and extend `ContestableEvidenceDetailsList` for the new evidence data.

Explains how to configure which evidence types citizens can contest in the citizen account for periodic data matching and annual renewals. By default, Death Status and MEC are contestable for periodic data matching, and income is contestable for annual renewals. Administrators can change this via the `curam.healthcare.pdm.contestable.evidences` property in the HEALTHCARE section. To add new contestable types, update the citizen account VIM file, override `HealthCarePDMProcessingImpl.java`, and extend `ContestableEvidenceDetailsList`.

children.title: Modifying periodic data matching home page messages
children.summary: This section explains how to change default messages shown in periodic data matching (PDM) home page and My Updates page. It covers editing DMX-based message sources (CITIZENHOMEMENUITEM, LOCALIZABLETEXT, TEXTTRANSLATION), creating new menu items and linking custom Java logic via CLASSNAME, and updating AnnualRenewal.properties / CitizenMessagesForPDM.properties with optional PDMMessagesEventListener overrides. It also notes that My Updates messages come from PDMUpdates.xml and can be customized by replacing the default HealthCarePDMProcessing implementation.

children.title: Modifying periodic data matching My Updates page messages
children.summary: This section explains how to change default messages shown on the periodic data matching My Updates page. It says the messages come from `EJBServer\components\HCROnline\message\PDMUpdates.xml` and are populated at runtime in the service layer. To customize them, modify `PDMUpdates.xml` and, if needed, replace the default `HealthCarePDMProcessingImpl` by implementing the `HealthCarePDMProcessing` interface.

children.title: Modifying annual renewals home page messages
children.summary: Explains how to customize annual renewals UI content. For the home page, it covers changing or creating messages via DMX tables/files (CITIZENHOMEMENUITEM, LOCALIZABLETEXT, TEXTTRANSLATION), updating the AnnualRenewalMenuItemProducer Java class or adding a new class, and modifying AnnualRenewal.properties through an AnnualRenewalMessageHelper implementation to populate PARTICIPANTMESSAGECONFIG. It also briefly notes My Updates page changes: implement/extend AnnualRenewalHelper, update evidence-related structs, and edit the CitizenAccount_arpdm.vim UI model for new clusters/columns.

children.title: Modifying the annual renewals My Updates page
children.summary: Explains how to customize the annual renewals My Updates page. By default it shows CASEEVIDENCE.EXTERNALINCOMEDETAILS and CASEEVIDENCE.ANNUALTAXRETURN evidence types. To add or change evidence types or columns, implement or extend AnnualRenewalHelper, update EvidenceTypeDetails and EvidenceDetails structs to support new evidence and visibility logic, and modify the CitizenAccount_arpdm.vim UI file to add columns or clusters. This section is a setup guide for extending the annual renewals update page data model and presentation.

This section explains how to customize the citizen account for periodic data matching and annual renewals. It covers configuring which evidence types are contestable (default: Death Status, MEC, and income) via the `curam.healthcare.pdm.contestable.evidences` property, plus required VIM and service-layer changes for new evidence. It also shows how to customize home page and My Updates messages for periodic data matching and annual renewals using DMX files, properties, helper/processing classes, and related UI model updates.

children.title: 12.16 Customizing evidence converters
External evidence converters
children.summary: Explains external evidence converters, which implement `ExternalEvidenceConverter` and are used by automatic-completion batch processes to turn polled external evidence into case evidence. A converter handles one polled evidence type per invocation and may create, modify, or both. It notes default converters for Death Status, MinimumEssentialCoverage, Annual Tax Return, and Income Details, with custom converters allowed for default or custom dynamic evidence types. Also mentions using `CommonExternalEvidenceConverter` and that default-created evidence uses the reason “Reported by External Party.”

children.title: Implementing a new external evidence converter
children.summary: Explains how to implement a new external evidence converter using the abstract CommonExternalEvidenceConverter helper. External evidence converters map one specific polled evidence type to case evidence, and are used by the ExternalEvidenceConverterManager in automatic-completion batch processes. The section outlines required steps: extend the helper with source/target types, implement abstract methods for source/target evidence types, create-details mapping, and modify-details mapping, and optionally override create/modify decision methods. Includes an example converter and notes that converters may create, modify, or end-date existing evidence.

children.title: Customizing an external evidence converter
children.summary: Explains how to customize or replace a default external evidence converter in Cúram. You can either extend the default converter or create a new implementation, then update Guice bindings so the external evidence converter manager uses the custom class instead of the default. If you add a new module, also update the ModuleClassname.dmx file to reference it.

children.title: Disabling an external evidence converter
children.summary: Explains how to disable an external evidence converter by replacing the default converter with a custom subclass whose convert() method returns no evidence. It outlines three steps: implement the no-op converter, add a Guice link binding in a module to substitute it for the default converter, and update ModuleClassname.dmx if a new module is created. Includes example code for an Annual Tax Return evidence converter.

Explains how to customize default evidence converters for periodic data matching/annual renewals. Covers external evidence converters that implement `ExternalEvidenceConverter` and convert one polled evidence type per invocation, creating or modifying case evidence via automatic-completion batch processes. Describes using `CommonExternalEvidenceConverter` to implement new converters, including required abstract methods and optional create/modify decisions. Also shows how to replace or disable default converters through Guice bindings and, if needed, updating `ModuleClassname.dmx`. Default mappings include Death Status, MEC, Annual Tax Return, and Income Details.

Covers implementing periodic data matching and annual renewals: one-time storage of existing program group determinations before upgrades, core workflow (poll external systems, add evidence, project eligibility, send notices, auto-complete), and bulk/run configuration setup. Also explains polling TDS sources, case selection, PDMEvidenceMaintenance usage, notice/APTC generation, batch execution and expiry intervals, error triage, and customization points for program group storage, completion, citizen account, and external evidence converters. Includes Guice/module binding examples and default converter mappings.

children.title: 13 Customizing inconsistency period processing
13.1 Creating a custom event handler for inconsistency period processing
children.summary: Explains how inconsistency period processing works and how to customize it with a custom event handler. Notes that the default HCR implementation creates an inconsistency period only once per case via `curam.hcrcase.sl.event.impl.MilestoneCreationEventHandler`, which reacts to an `INCONSISTENCYPERIOD` event raised by `ProgramGroupManager.manageProgramGroup(CaseKey)`. The handler checks milestone configuration and creates a milestone only when none exists and the milestone is in `INPROGRESS` or `NONSTARTED` state. Also points to `handler_config.xml` event registration and related customization topics.

children.title: 13.2 InconsistencyPeriod workflow
children.summary: The InconsistencyPeriod workflow processes a milestone delivery after the inconsistency period ends. It checks for outstanding verifications or issues on the Insurance Affordability integrated case. If none exist, it completes the milestone. If they do exist, it verifies whether external evidence (for example, from the federal hub) is available. If evidence is present, it extends the waiver date by 10 days, copies external evidence to client-attested evidence, updates ClntAttestModifiedEvidence, sends a potential eligibility notification, and completes. If evidence is missing, it suspends the product delivery cases and completes.

children.title: 13.3 Inconsistency period workflow APIs
children.summary: Lists the default APIs used by the inconsistency period workflow. These include checks for outstanding verifications/issues, checking external system evidence availability (incarceration, ESI, MEC, external income details), extending waiver dates via a configurable environment setting, overriding client-attested evidence from external evidence, sending potential eligibility notifications, suspending product delivery cases, and ending the milestone. It also introduces the related batch process that activates evidence created or modified by this workflow by reading ClntAttestModifiedEvidence and updating evidence status.

children.title: 13.4 Inconsistency Period Evidence Activation batch process
children.summary: Describes the “Inconsistency Period Evidence Activation” batch process, which activates evidence created or modified by the Inconsistency Period workflow. It reads cases from ClntAttestModifiedEvidence for a specified date, then activates the created/modified evidence and updates the record status in ClntAttestModifiedEvidence. Useful for understanding post-workflow evidence activation and persistence updates.

children.title: 13.5 Inconsistency Period Evidence Activation Stream batch process
children.summary: This section describes the 13.5 Inconsistency Period Evidence Activation Stream batch process. It supports streaming for inconsistency period Insurance Affordability cases. The only parameter listed is processingDate, which uses the current date by default and has no applicable default value.

Covers customization of inconsistency period processing for Insurance Affordability cases. Explains the default flow: Program Group Manager raises an INCONSISTENCYPERIOD event, a milestone is created only once by the default handler, and the InconsistencyPeriod workflow handles end-of-period processing. Lists the workflow APIs for checking outstanding verifications, external evidence, extending waiver dates, copying client-attested evidence, notifying clients, suspending product delivery cases, and ending milestones. Also documents the evidence activation batch and stream batch processes that activate modified evidence from ClntAttestModifiedEvidence.

children.title: 14 Configuring Account Transfer with the Federally Facilitated Exchange
14.1 The FederalExchange component
children.summary: The FederalExchange component supports Account Transfer processing between Cúram and the Federally Facilitated Exchange (FFE). It handles incoming FFE applications for state agencies and sends state-originated applications back to the FFE when applicants are not eligible for Medicaid or CHIP. The section also notes that Account Transfer source code is delivered with Cúram, uses the data store and Persistence Infrastructure, and maps inbound payloads to the Intake datastore using only pre-existing datastore evidence entities.

children.title: 14.2 Configuring Federal Exchange
Activating Account Transfer
children.summary: Explains how to activate Account Transfer and control its processing. By default it is off; setting `curam.healthcare.account.transfer.activate.outbound.mapping` to `true` enables eligibility determinations to be prepared for the FFE via FederalExchange. It also covers batch mode (`curam.healthcare.account.transfer.processing.mode=batch`) to prevent inbound/outbound processing, and disabling automatic submission to Cúram with `curam.healthcare.account.transfer.auto.submit=false`, leaving applications in `PENDING` on `FederalExchangeApplication`.

children.title: Enabling batch processing of account transfer applications
children.summary: Explains how to enable batch processing for Account Transfer applications. Account Transfer is off by default; activating it processes eligibility determinations for the Federal Exchange. To use batch mode, set `curam.healthcare.account.transfer.processing.mode` from `online` to `batch`, which stops data mapping/processing and stores pending jobs in the `FederalExchangeApplication` entity. Also covers stopping submissions to Cúram by setting `curam.healthcare.account.transfer.auto.submit` from `true` to `false`, leaving applications in `PENDING` state.

children.title: Configuring the sending of Account Transfers to Cúram
children.summary: Explains how to control Account Transfer processing for Cúram. It covers activating outbound Account Transfer via the `curam.healthcare.account.transfer.activate.outbound.mapping` property, switching processing from online to batch with `curam.healthcare.account.transfer.processing.mode`, and preventing submissions to Cúram by setting `curam.healthcare.account.transfer.auto.submit` to false. Notes that when processing is stopped, applications remain in `PENDING` on the `FederalExchangeApplication` entity.

children.title: Selecting the source data set for outbound mapping
children.summary: Explains how to configure outbound Account Transfer mapping source data. Two source datasets are available: Intake data from the internal case worker intake app, or Case processing data derived by HCRDatastoreBuilder from case/person evidence. Requires setting the outbound mapping source property and matching internal datastore schema. Also covers configuring sender identity via US state code, county, and category properties, and setting Account Transfer agency type to Medicaid, CHIP, or both (default B).

children.title: Setting the identity of the sender US state
children.summary: Explains how to configure the sender’s US state for Account Transfer in HCR. The required state identifiers are set through properties for sender state code, county, and category code, and should match the state where HCR is implemented. This page also includes related configuration for selecting the outbound mapping source data set (intake or case processing) and setting the Account Transfer agency type (M, C, or B).

children.title: Setting the Account Transfer agency type
children.summary: Configures the Account Transfer agency type for Health Care Reform. Update `curam.healthcare.account.transfer.agency.type` to set whether Account Transfer applies to Medicaid (`M`), CHIP (`C`), or both (`B`), with `B` as the default.

children.title: Setting the federal exchange code
children.summary: Configures Account Transfer integration properties: sets the federal exchange code/name used in inbound and outbound requests; links the Datastore schema name to the Account Transfer person reference and mapped person reference; sets the FFE data store schema name; and defines inbound Account Transfer date/time formats via application properties for date and datetime mapping.

children.title: Linking the Datastore schema name to the Account Transfer person reference
children.summary: Configure the Datastore schema name used to link Account Transfer external person references to Cúram person references. Update `curam.healthcare.account.transfer.person.link.schema` with the Datastore schema name; this same schema name is used for the mapped person reference after data mapping. This section sits among related Account Transfer properties for federal exchange codes, FFE schema naming, and date/time format configuration.

children.title: Setting the data store schema name for the FFE schema
children.summary: Explains how to configure the data store schema name for the FFE schema by setting the `curam.healthcare.fedexchange.version.schema` property. This page also includes nearby Account Transfer configuration topics: federal exchange code, person reference schema linking, and date/time format properties for inbound Account Transfers.

children.title: Configuring Account Transfer date and time formats
children.summary: Configures date and time formats for inbound and outbound Account Transfers to keep payloads and target systems consistent. The section explains how to set inbound Account Transfer formatting via application properties, including curam.healthcare.account.transfer.mapping.curam.date.format for datastore date creation from FFM dates and curam.healthcare.account.transfer.mapping.curam.datetime.format for Cúram datetime creation from FFM datetimes.

Configures Account Transfer with the Federally Facilitated Exchange via application properties. Covers activating outbound processing, batch mode, and preventing auto-submit to Cúram (leaving applications PENDING in FederalExchangeApplication). Also explains outbound mapping source selection (intake vs case processing), sender US state identifiers, agency type (M/C/B), federal exchange code, person-reference schema linking, FFE schema name, and inbound date/datetime formats. Useful for setting up or adjusting FFE integration behavior and data mapping.

children.title: 14.3 Extending Federally Facilitated Exchange data mappings
Adding or updating the attributes for a data store entity
children.summary: Explains how to extend Federally Facilitated Exchange (FFE) data mappings by adding or updating attributes on a data store entity. After the FederalExchange component maps an entity, a Persistence Infrastructure event lets custom listeners modify entity attributes, but any added attributes must match the configured data store schema. The section points to the relevant event properties for inbound and outbound custom mapping: `curam.hcr.fedexchange.mapper.impl.EntityMapper.MapEvent.customInboundMap` and `.customOutboundMap`, and notes that event signature details are in the Javadoc.

children.title: Adding an entity as a child of a mapped data store entity
children.summary: Explains how to add child entities during data store entity mapping via custom listener events. When an entity is mapped and children are being added, an event is fired so listeners can append extra child entities. Notes that any added child type must already exist in the data store schema. Points to the relevant event configuration for inbound and outbound mapping, including the customOutboundMapChildren path.

children.title: Adding or replacing a top-level data store entity
children.summary: Describes how to add custom processing for top-level data store entities via element mapping providers. It explains that these mappings typically apply to a single-instance child of the Application root or similar entity, and shows the relevant extension events for inbound and outbound mapping: `ElementMapperEvent.addElementMapperEvent(Map elementMappers)` and `FFEElementMapperEvent.addFFEElementMapperEvent(Map elementMappers)`. Also notes that custom listeners can append entity types and mapping implementations to the provider’s map.

children.title: Adding or updating entities for an outbound response to the FFE
children.summary: Explains how to add or update outbound entities in a response sent to the FFE/FFM after FederalExchange builds the response. The process uses the event `curam.hcr.fedexchange.mapper.impl.EntityMapper.MapEvent.customOutboundMapResponse`, which passes the mapped parent entity and original XML element so listeners can modify or create response data before transmission. Includes an example listener signature and notes custom processing should check for an existing response entity before creating one.

Explains how to extend Federally Facilitated Exchange (FFE) mappings for inbound and outbound data. Covers four extension points: add/update attributes on mapped data store entities, add child entities to mapped entities, add/replace top-level entities via element mapping providers, and modify outbound response entities before sending to the FFE. Notes that custom data must match the configured data store schema, child types must already exist, and relevant events/Javadoc signatures are provided for inbound and outbound custom listeners.

children.title: 14.4 The Web Service Java API
Inbound Account Transfer payload processing
Inbound payload identification and routing
children.summary: This section explains how inbound Account Transfer/COC payloads are identified and routed using the global application ID and original signature date. It describes that the inbound web service passes payloads to the Java API, persists them to the federalexchangemessagelog table, and may create an Account Transfer COC Application Case. It also notes how duplicate application IDs with different signature dates are treated as separate change-of-circumstance payloads requiring separate processing.

children.title: Inbound payload mapping configuration
children.summary: Explains how to configure inbound Account Transfer COC payload mapping using XML mapping files, including data-type conversions between CMS and Cúram, defaulting/validation behavior, flexible Date vs DateTime handling, dependency-aware mapping, boolean conversion, and custom enumerators for code-table mapping. Notes that the initial application date maps to the evidence start date and lists the relevant ATCOCEvidenceMappingConfiguration XML files. Also describes using the Java API `AccountTransferWS.initiateAccountTransfer` to batch-process pending COC payloads.

children.title: Process inbound Account Transfer COC payloads in batch
children.summary: Explains how inbound Account Transfer COC payloads are handled in batch. It describes duplicate detection rules: if multiple payloads share the same application ID and original signature date, only the first is processed; payloads with an already processed global application ID and transfer ID are ignored. It also notes that batch processing uses the `curam.hcr.fedexchange.ws.impl.AccountTransferWS.initiateAccountTransfer` API to take Pending COC transfers and pass them for processing.

Describes inbound Account Transfer payload handling via `AccountTransferWS.initiateAccountTransfer`, including web service-to-Java API routing, persistence to `federalexchangemessagelog`, and creation of an Account Transfer COC Application Case for inbound COC payloads. Explains identification/duplicate rules using global application ID, transfer ID, and original signature date: same app ID with different signature dates are separate COC payloads; same app ID and signature date processes only the first; exact duplicates are ignored. Also covers XML mapping configuration, datatype/defaulting/validation behavior, code-table enumerators, and batch processing of pending COC transfers.

children.title: Outbound processing
children.summary: Describes outbound Account Transfer processing to the Federally Facilitated Exchange (FFE) using web service events. Key API events are `OutBoundDataEvent.sendOutBoundTransferDataEvent` and `OutBoundResponseEvent.sendOutBoundResponseEvent`, which let listeners modify payloads before sending and apply custom mapping updates during data mapping or outbound response processing. Also introduces `HCRFedExchangeAppStatus` status codes for `FederalExchangeApplication`, including `IBD_IN_PROGRESS`, `IBD_UPDATE_PENDING`, and `IBD_ACKNOWLEDGED`, with notes on when each status is assigned.

children.title: HCRFedExchangeAppStatus code table descriptions
children.summary: Describes the HCRFedExchangeAppStatus code table for FederalExchangeApplication records, focusing on inbound Account Transfer from FFM to the State Medicaid Agency. It explains the status sequence and meanings: IBD_IN_PROGRESS for initial creation, IBD_UPDATE_PENDING when an existing Global Application ID indicates a change of circumstance, and IBD_ACKNOWLEDGED after successful storage and acknowledgment. The surrounding text also notes batch processing and outbound event hooks for sending Account Transfer data and responses to the FFE.

Explains the Web Service Java API for Account Transfer inbound/outbound integration with the FFE. Covers `AccountTransferWS.initiateAccountTransfer` for inbound payload routing, persistence to `federalexchangemessagelog`, creation of Account Transfer COC cases, duplicate/COC identification rules using global application ID, transfer ID, and signature date, and batch processing of pending COC records. Also describes XML mapping customization, datatype/defaulting/validation, enumerators, and outbound events for sending transfer data/responses. Includes `HCRFedExchangeAppStatus` states: `IBD_IN_PROGRESS`, `IBD_UPDATE_PENDING`, `IBD_ACKNOWLEDGED`.

children.title: 14.5 Adding a new entity
Writing an EntityMapper
children.summary: Explains how to write an EntityMapper for each new entity in Cúram Federal Exchange configuration. An EntityMapper must implement curam.hcr.fedexchange.mapper.impl.EntityMapper, and can use ElementMapperUtil in map() to find required XML elements and attributes. The section instructs you to implement the mapper from the example, then register it with the appropriate ElementMapperEvent inbound or outbound event. The example shows mapping IncomeItem entities from FederalExchange XML into Cúram datastore entities, setting fields, and adding them as children.

children.title: Updating the Federal Exchange data store schema
children.summary: Explains how to update the Federal Exchange data store schema when adding a new entity for Account Transfer inbound/outbound mapping. Notes two schema requirements: element text from payloads must be stored via a `value` attribute, and namespaced payload elements may need a `nameSpacePrefix` attribute with a default value. Identifies the schema via the `curam.healthcare.fedexchange.version.schema` property and instructs updating it with the new entity. Also points to default Account Transfer workflows viewable in Administration Workspace/Process Definition Tool.

Explains how to add a new mapped entity to Account Transfer/Federal Exchange. For each entity, implement a custom `curam.hcr.fedexchange.mapper.impl.EntityMapper`, using `ElementMapperUtil` in `map()` to find source XML elements/attributes, create target datastore entities, set fields, and attach them to the parent. Then register the mapper with the appropriate `ElementMapperEvent` for inbound or outbound processing. Also requires updating the Federal Exchange data store schema to include the new entity.

children.title: 14.6 Account transfer workflows
children.summary: Explains how to update the Federal Exchange data store schema for Account Transfer, including handling XML element text as attributes and namespace prefixes as default attributes. Identifies the schema via the curam.healthcare.fedexchange.version.schema property and instructs updating it for new entities. Also describes default Account Transfer workflows in Administration Workspace/Process Definition Tool: Account Transfer Inbound COC, which raises tasks for evidence needing attention and can be customized, and Account Transfer Straight Through Authorise, used when no evidence needs attention or only person participants exist with no open duplicate COC applications.

Covers configuring and extending Account Transfer integration with the Federally Facilitated Exchange (FFE). Topics include activating outbound processing, batch mode, auto-submit control, source selection (intake vs case processing), sender/state and agency properties, exchange/person-link/schema settings, and inbound/outbound date formats. Explains how FFE mappings can be customized via persistence events and entity mappers, how to add new entities and update the data store schema, and the Java API, status codes, and workflows for inbound COC and straight-through authorization.

children.title: 15 Monitoring Health Care Reform
15.1 Monitoring HCR applications
HCR application intake process overview
children.summary: Overview of the HCR application intake flow from submission to product delivery case creation. It explains dynamic questionnaire submission, external data verification, creation of an application case, and two paths: straight-through processing or manual processing. It notes resilient intake handling via the curam.intake.use.resilience property, automatic authorization, deferred evidence sharing to integrated cases, and the HCR Process Instance Errors view for technical failures. It also covers partial evidence brokering and required manual intervention when sharing fails.

children.title: (deprecated) Monitoring HCR intake reports
(deprecated) HCR Intake Reports
children.summary: Deprecated HCR Intake Reports show a business view of HCR applications processed by the system, from initiation to eligibility decision. They summarize application counts by state and source, received vs submitted totals, interventions required, and eligible programs since a chosen date. The section explains categories such as outstanding registrations, verifications, failed validations, failed eligibility, and failed evidence brokering, and warns that refreshing reports can heavily load the system. It also notes technical-error handling via HCR Intake Process Errors and lists default program abbreviations.

Deprecated HCR Intake Reports let administrators monitor HCR applications from initiation to eligibility decision. They show counts by state and source, received vs submitted totals, intervention-needed cases, and eligible programs since a chosen date (default: one week back). Intervention categories include outstanding registrations/verifications, failed validations, failed eligibility, and failed evidence brokering. Technical errors are surfaced via HCR Intake Process Errors. Refreshing reports can heavily load the system and block intake processing.

children.title: Monitoring HCR intake process instance errors
children.summary: Explains how to monitor HCR Intake process instance errors in Cúram Administration. The HCR Intake Process Errors view provides an intake-focused summary of workflow and deferred process errors, showing background processes used in intake and the number requiring technical intervention. Workflow charts show only unresolved errors in the selected period; deferred processing charts show all errors in that period. Users can drill into each count to view individual errors and perform root cause analysis. Notes that some processes are shared outside Intake and that these reports depend on the curam.intake.use.resilience property.

Covers how to monitor HCR applications in Cúram. Explains the intake flow from questionnaire submission through external data verification, application case creation, straight-through vs manual processing, resilient mode via curam.intake.use.resilience, automatic authorization, and evidence sharing to integrated cases. Notes how partial/full evidence brokering failures are handled and when caseworker intervention is needed. Also describes the deprecated HCR Intake Reports for tracking application states, intervention categories, and eligible programs, with a warning that refreshing reports can heavily load the system.

children.title: 15.2 Monitoring Cúram processes
Monitoring workflow process instances
children.summary: Explains how to monitor and troubleshoot workflow process instances in Cúram Administration. Use Process Instances to view current workflow status via search/filtering, and manage in-progress instances by suspending/resuming, stopping (aborting), or overriding event waits. Also covers Process Instance Errors for reviewing workflow/deferred process errors, regularly checking for operational issues, viewing error details, and retrying or aborting failed instances; retry re-enacts the process from the failure point.

children.title: Monitoring Process Instance Errors
Process Instance Errors
children.summary: Explains how to monitor workflow/process instance errors in Cúram Administration via Process Monitoring > Process Instance Errors, using search/filtering and error details to investigate failures. The Workflow Engine records error information for troubleshooting deferred or workflow process instances. Administrators can retry a failed instance to rerun it from the failure point or abort it to stop the instance and associated activities/tasks, though some manual cleanup may still be required.

Explains how administrators monitor workflow and deferred process instance errors in Cúram Administration via Administration Workspace > Process Monitoring > Process Instance Errors. You can search and filter errors, open error details, and use the Workflow Engine’s recorded error information to troubleshoot failures. Failed workflow process instances can be retried to restart from the failure point or aborted to stop the instance, associated activities, and related tasks, though some manual cleanup may still be needed.

Explains how to monitor and troubleshoot Cúram process instances and errors through Administration Workspace > Process Monitoring. Use Process Instances to search/filter workflow status and manage in-progress instances by suspending, resuming, stopping, or overriding event waits. Use Process Instance Errors to review workflow and deferred process failures, inspect error details, and retry or abort failed workflow instances. The section also notes that monitoring should be done regularly for operational issues.

Monitors Health Care Reform intake and related workflow health in Cúram. Covers application intake flow from questionnaire submission, external verification, application case creation, straight-through vs manual authorization, resilient mode, automatic evidence sharing, and handling of partial/full brokering failures. Includes deprecated HCR Intake Reports for tracking application states, intervention types, and eligible programs, with a warning that refreshes are system-heavy. Also explains HCR Process Instance Errors and general Process Monitoring views for workflow/deferred errors, plus suspend/resume/stop, override waits, retry, and abort actions.

children.title: 16 Running a bulk reassessment of all open integrated cases
16.1 BulkICReassessment
children.summary: BulkICReassessment is a batch process that performs a full reassessment of many open Income Support for Medical Assistance (Health Care Reform) integrated cases. It is used when changes affect numerous cases; updated determinations are stored and old ones superseded. By default it runs on all open eligible cases, so a custom case selection strategy may be needed to limit scope. The section lists the process class/method, the Batch Process Instance ID parameter, and related bulk stream settings for concurrent execution and retry intervals.

children.title: 16.2 BulkICReassessmentStream
children.summary: Describes the BulkICReassessmentStream batch process, which provides streaming support for bulk integrated case reassessment in HCR and runs only with the BulkICReassessment batch process. Explains how to link one or more stream processes to a specific batch process instance using the Batch Process Instance ID parameter, including matching the same ID across linked processes. Also lists the related class/method, curam.healthcare.sl.intf.BulkICReassessmentStream.process, and notes that the instance ID enables multiple concurrent batch process instances.

Describes the BulkICReassessment batch process for full reassessment of many open HCR integrated cases after system changes, with updated determinations stored and old ones superseded. By default it runs on all open eligible cases, so custom case selection may be needed. Covers report output, customization hooks, environment settings, and concurrent chunk/stream execution. Also documents the BulkICReassessmentStream process, which can run only with BulkICReassessment and must share the same Batch Process Instance ID to support multiple concurrent instances and linked streams.

children.title: 17 Customizing the intake process
17.1 Address mapping
children.summary: Explains how to customize address mapping in the Health Care Reform intake process. It says to extend `curam.healthcare.intake.impl.AddressDataIntakeApplicationListener`, which is used during pre-mapping when an HCR IEG script is submitted from the portal or caseworker application to map address data from the data store to case evidence. Includes an example override of `preMapDataToCuram` and shows binding the custom class in a Guice module.

Explains how to customize the default Health Care Reform intake process, specifically address mapping. It recommends extending `curam.healthcare.intake.impl.AddressDataIntakeApplicationListener`, which handles pre-mapping when an HCR IEG script is submitted from the portal or caseworker application and maps address data from the datastore to case evidence. Includes a sample override of `preMapDataToCuram` and shows binding the custom listener in a Guice module.
